text:
Base directory for this skill: /home/Axel/LeanAlgebraicGeometry-Horizon/.claude/skills/blueprint-conventions

Blueprints are a LaTeX subset (the `leanblueprint` dialect) parsed into the DAG.
Write timeless mathematics — a standalone document, not a project journal.

## Environments

Each declaration is an amsthm-style environment; the recognized ones are
`theorem`, `lemma`, `proposition`, `corollary`, `definition`, `remark`,
`example`, `notation`, `convention`, and `proof`. A statement environment plus
its following `proof` environment form one node's content.

## Per-node macros

- `\label{id}` — a stable id (the DAG node key).
- `\lean{Name}` — the Lean declaration this node formalizes. Keep it 1-to-1: one
  blueprint node ↔ one Lean declaration, signatures matching.
- `\uses{a,b}` — dependencies on other nodes' labels; these drive the DAG edges.
  Add an edge instead of re-explaining a dependency in prose. Put `\uses` in the
  statement for definitional deps and in the `proof` for deps used only in the
  proof.
- `\source{slug:page-0001}` — reference anchors for UI popups and audit trails.
  Use page-level ids from `references/<slug>/tex/page-0001.tex` or an equivalent
  manifest entry. Multiple anchors may be comma-separated.
- `\leanok` — the Lean is written and checked. Honest only when its Lean is
  actually checked *and* its dependencies are too. On a `proof`, it means the
  proof is formalized; on a statement, the signature is formalized.
- `\mathlibok` — the declaration is already available in mathlib (no
  formalization needed; treat as a leaf).

## House style

- **Pure mathematics only.** No Lean tactics, typeclass/implementation notes, or
  semi-Lean pseudocode. No project history ("since iter N", "our failed route")
  and no conversational filler. If a Lean helper exists with no math counterpart,
  write its LaTeX statement so the 1-to-1 correspondence holds.
- **Complete proofs, not sketches.** A node's `proof` is a real, rigorous
  mathematical proof a reader could check — not a hand-wave or a TODO. The way to
  keep it short is to *split*, not to abbreviate: if a proof is long or a node
  grows past a short paragraph, break out the hard step as its own `\uses`-linked
  lemma (down to sentence-sized lemmas if needed) so each node stays small and
  its proof stays complete.
- **One declaration's worth per node.** Don't restate Lean source as prose.
- **Cite by source link, not quote comments.** Add `\source{...}` to the
  relevant node. Keep the retrieved/transcribed page text in `references/` (for
  example `references/<slug>/tex/page-0042.tex`) and let the UI expose it; do not
  paste verbatim source quotes into `% QUOTE` comments inside the blueprint.

2293	\section{Support and freeness predicates}
2294	\label{sec:quot_predicates}
2295	
2296	The Quot functor (\cref{def:quot_functor}) and the Grassmannian
2297	(\cref{def:grassmannian_scheme}) classify quotient sheaves subject to two
2298	geometric conditions: a coherent quotient must have \emph{schematic support
2299	proper over the base}, and the Grassmannian's quotients must be \emph{locally
2300	free of a fixed rank}. This section introduces both predicates.
2301	
2302	\begin{definition}
2303	
2304	\leanok
2305	[Annihilator ideal sheaf of a sheaf of modules]
2306	  \label{def:modules_annihilator}
2307	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.annihilator}
2308	  % NOTE: The definition carries no \uses: the Lean construction is
2309	  % `IdealSheafData.ofIdeals (fun U => Module.annihilator Γ(X,U) Γ(F,U))`, which is well-defined
2310	  % unconditionally and does NOT depend on the localization bridges at definition time. The two
2311	  % facts lem:annihilator_localization_eq_map and lem:qcoh_section_localization_basicOpen are needed
2312	  % only for the downstream CHARACTERIZATION (the reverse inclusion / equality on affine opens, the
2313	  % "annihilator forward direction"), which is not yet a blueprint block.
2314	  % NOTE: The definition itself is formalized and axiom-clean; the inclusion direction
2315	  % \(\mathrm{Ann}(\mathcal{F})(U) \subseteq \mathrm{Ann}_{\mathcal{O}_X(U)}(\mathcal{F}(U))\)
2316	  % (\cref{lem:modules_annihilator_ideal_le}) is closed. The reverse inclusion, which identifies
2317	  % the annihilator sheaf with the module-annihilator on affine opens, remains bridge-gated on
2318	  % \cref{lem:qcoh_section_localization_basicOpen}.
2319	  % SOURCE: [Nitsure], \S 1, sub-section "Stratification by Hilbert
2320	  % Polynomials" (read from
2321	  % references/nitsure-hilbert-quot-src/nitsure-hilbert-quot.tex, L468-L471).
2322	  % SOURCE QUOTE:
2323	  % "Let $X\to S$ be a finite type morphism of noetherian schemes,
2324	  %  and let $L$ be a line bundle on $X$. Let $\F$
2325	  %  be any coherent sheaf on
2326	  %  $X$ whose schematic support is proper over $S$."
2327	  \textit{Source: [Nitsure], \S 1 (FGA Explained Ch.~5).}
2328	  Let \(X\) be a scheme and let \(\mathcal{F}\) be a sheaf of
2329	  \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules that is \emph{quasi-coherent and of finite type}
2330	  (over a locally noetherian base, so that on each affine open \(U\) the section
2331	  module \(\mathcal{F}(U)\) is a finitely generated \(\mathcal{O}_X(U)\)-module).
2332	  The \emph{annihilator ideal sheaf} of \(\mathcal{F}\) is the ideal sheaf
2333	  \(\mathrm{Ann}(\mathcal{F})\) defined on affine opens: for each affine open
2334	  \(U \subseteq X\),
2335	  \[
2336	    \mathrm{Ann}(\mathcal{F})(U)
2337	    \;=\; \mathrm{Ann}_{\mathcal{O}_X(U)}\bigl(\mathcal{F}(U)\bigr)
2338	    \;\subseteq\; \mathcal{O}_X(U),
2339	  \]
2340	  the annihilator of the \(\mathcal{O}_X(U)\)-module \(\mathcal{F}(U)\). These
2341	  local data patch to a coherent ideal sheaf on \(X\) whose vanishing locus is
2342	  the set-theoretic support of \(\mathcal{F}\).
2343	
2344	  \emph{Well-definedness on basic opens.} The coherence condition for an ideal
2345	  sheaf requires that, for an affine open \(U\) and \(f \in \mathcal{O}_X(U)\),
2346	  the section \(\mathrm{Ann}(\mathcal{F})(U)\) restricts correctly to the basic
2347	  open \(D(f) \subseteq U\), i.e. that
2348	  \(\mathrm{Ann}_{\mathcal{O}_X(D(f))}(\mathcal{F}(D(f)))\) is the extension of
2349	  \(\mathrm{Ann}_{\mathcal{O}_X(U)}(\mathcal{F}(U))\) along the localization map
2350	  \(\mathcal{O}_X(U) \to \mathcal{O}_X(D(f))\). This is exactly where the two
2351	  hypotheses enter. The bridge
2352	  \cref{lem:qcoh_section_localization_basicOpen} supplies, for a quasi-coherent
2353	  finite-type \(\mathcal{F}\), that \(\mathcal{O}_X(D(f)) = (\mathcal{O}_X(U))_f\)
2354	  and that the restriction \(\mathcal{F}(U) \to \mathcal{F}(D(f))\) exhibits
2355	  \(\mathcal{F}(D(f))\) as the localized module \((\mathrm{powers}\,f)^{-1}
2356	  \mathcal{F}(U)\); the algebra engine
2357	  \cref{lem:annihilator_localization_eq_map} then yields, using that
2358	  \(\mathcal{F}(U)\) is finitely generated, the required identity
2359	  \(\mathrm{Ann}_{(\mathcal{O}_X(U))_f}\bigl((\mathrm{powers}\,f)^{-1}
2360	  \mathcal{F}(U)\bigr) = \mathrm{Ann}_{\mathcal{O}_X(U)}(\mathcal{F}(U)) \cdot
2361	  (\mathcal{O}_X(U))_f\). Thus the local annihilators are compatible with basic-open
2362	  restriction, which is the patching datum that makes \(\mathrm{Ann}(\mathcal{F})\)
2363	  a well-defined ideal sheaf. The finite-type hypothesis is genuinely required:
2364	  without finite generation of \(\mathcal{F}(U)\) the annihilator need not commute
2365	  with localization, and the local data fail to patch.
2366	
2367	  \emph{Note.} Mathlib does not carry an annihilator for sheaves of modules on a scheme.
2368	\end{definition}

2371	[Annihilator ideal bounded by the module annihilator on affine opens]
2372	  \label{lem:modules_annihilator_ideal_le}
2373	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.annihilator_ideal_le}
2374	  \uses{def:modules_annihilator}
2375	  Let \(X\) be a scheme, \(\mathcal{F}\) a sheaf of \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules and
2376	  \(U \subseteq X\) an affine open. Then the section over \(U\) of the annihilator
2377	  ideal sheaf \(\mathrm{Ann}(\mathcal{F})\) (\cref{def:modules_annihilator}) is
2378	  contained in the module-annihilator of the sections of \(\mathcal{F}\) over
2379	  \(U\):
2380	  \[
2381	    \mathrm{Ann}(\mathcal{F})(U)
2382	    \;\subseteq\; \mathrm{Ann}_{\mathcal{O}_X(U)}\bigl(\mathcal{F}(U)\bigr).
2383	  \]
2384	  This is the inclusion direction available directly from the definition of
2385	  \(\mathrm{Ann}(\mathcal{F})\); the reverse inclusion is the basic-open coherence
2386	  statement gated on \cref{lem:qcoh_section_localization_basicOpen}. Proved directly
2387	  in Lean. It supports the well-definedness of the schematic support
2388	  (\cref{def:schematic_support}).

2393	  \label{lem:isLocalization_basicOpen_mathlib}
2394	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.IsAffineOpen.isLocalization_basicOpen}
2395	  \mathlibok

2398	  Let \(X\) be a scheme, \(U \subseteq X\) an affine open, and
2399	  \(f \in \Gamma(X, U)\). Then the restriction map
2400	  \(\Gamma(X, U) \to \Gamma\bigl(X,\, D(f)\bigr)\) on the basic open
2401	  \(D(f) = X.\mathrm{basicOpen}\,f\) exhibits \(\Gamma(X, D(f))\) as the
2402	  localization of \(\Gamma(X, U)\) away from \(f\); that is, it is
2403	  \(\mathrm{IsLocalization.Away}\,f\), so
2404	  \(\Gamma(X, D(f)) = (\Gamma(X, U))_f\) as a \(\Gamma(X,U)\)-algebra.
2405	\end{lemma}

2414	\begin{lemma}[Sheaf Mayer--Vietoris: the pullback cone over a two-open cover is a limit]
2415	  \label{lem:isLimitPullbackCone_mathlib}
2416	  \lean{TopCat.Sheaf.isLimitPullbackCone}
2417	  \mathlibok

2421	  \(F(U \cup V)\), \(F(U)\), \(F(V)\), \(F(U \cap V)\) is a pullback (limit). This is the generic

2426	\begin{lemma}[Separatedness: a section is determined by its restrictions to a cover]
2427	  \label{lem:eq_of_locally_eq_mathlib}
2428	  \lean{TopCat.Sheaf.eq_of_locally_eq'}
2429	  \mathlibok

2432	  Let \(F\) be a sheaf on a topological space, \(V\) an open, and \(\{U_i\}\) a family of opens with
2433	  \(\bigsqcup_i U_i = V\). If two sections \(s, t \in F(V)\) have equal restrictions
2434	  \(s|_{U_i} = t|_{U_i}\) for every \(i\), then \(s = t\). This separatedness over a cover is the
2435	  uniqueness half of the sheaf condition; it discharges the \(\mathrm{exists\_of\_eq}\) obligation
2436	  (a global section vanishing on \(D(f)\) is killed by a power of \(f\)) and the final
2437	  ``conclude on \(D(f)\)'' step of the cover-form descent

2452	  \label{lem:modules_pullback_mathlib}

2456	  For a morphism of schemes \(f : X \to Y\), Mathlib supplies the pullback functor
2457	  \(f^{*} : Y.\mathrm{Modules} \to X.\mathrm{Modules}\) on sheaves of modules, left adjoint to
2458	  pushforward, hence colimit-preserving, and carrying the unit sheaf to the unit sheaf. The project

2469	  \(f^{\mathrm{res}} : Y.\mathrm{Modules} \to X.\mathrm{Modules}\), with

2499	\begin{lemma}[Slice of opens equivalent to opens of the open subspace]
2500	  \label{lem:opens_overEquivalence_mathlib}
2501	  \lean{TopologicalSpace.Opens.overEquivalence}
2502	  \mathlibok
2503	  \textit{Provided by Mathlib (\texttt{Mathlib.Topology.Sheaves.Over}).}
2504	  For a topological space \(X\) and an open \(U \subseteq X\), the slice category of opens of
2505	  \(X\) over \(U\) (the opens of \(X\) contained in \(U\), with inclusions) is equivalent to the
2506	  opens of the subspace \(U\):
2507	  \((\mathrm{Opens}\,X)_{/U} \simeq \mathrm{Opens}(U)\). Mathlib supplies this underlying

2512	  \label{lem:equivalence_sheafCongr_mathlib}

2515	  \textit{Provided by Mathlib (\texttt{Mathlib.CategoryTheory.Sites.Equivalence}).}
2516	  Let \(e : C \simeq D\) be an equivalence of categories carrying Grothendieck topologies \(J\)
2517	  on \(C\) and \(K\) on \(D\), and suppose \(e.\mathrm{inverse}\) is a dense subsite for
2518	  \((K, J)\). Then for any target category \(A\) the sheaf categories are equivalent:

2537	\begin{lemma}\leanok
2538	[The over-equivalence functor is cocontinuous]
2539	  \label{lem:overEquivalence_functor_isCocontinuous}
2540	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.overEquivalence_functor_isCocontinuous}
2541	  \uses{lem:opens_overEquivalence_mathlib}
2542	  \textit{Topological layer of bridge C; fills the \texttt{Topology/Sheaves/Over.lean} TODO.}
2543	  The functor of \(\mathrm{Opens.overEquivalence}\,U\) (\cref{lem:opens_overEquivalence_mathlib})
2544	  is cocontinuous (cover-lifting) from the \(U\)-slice \(J_X|_U\) of the opens Grothendieck
2545	  topology of \(X\) to the opens Grothendieck topology \(J_U\) of the subspace \(U\).
2546	\end{lemma}
2547	\begin{proof}
2548	  \uses{lem:opens_overEquivalence_mathlib}
2549	  A sieve covers in the slice topology \(J_X|_U\) iff its image sieve covers in \(J_X\), and
2550	  covering for the opens topology is pointwise: a sieve covers iff its members' opens cover the
2551	  base set-theoretically. Given a point \(x\) of a slice object and a cover member in \(J_U\)
2552	  containing the image of \(x\), transport it across the open embedding \(U \hookrightarrow X\),
2553	  which is injective: the set-theoretic image of an open \(V \subseteq U\) is an open of \(X\)
2554	  contained in the base, a neighbourhood of \(x\) lying in the image sieve. This witnesses the
2555	  cover-lifting condition.
2556	\end{proof}
2557	
2558	\begin{lemma}\leanok
2559	[The over-equivalence inverse is cocontinuous]
2560	  \label{lem:overEquivalence_inverse_isCocontinuous}
2561	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.overEquivalence_inverse_isCocontinuous}
2562	  \uses{lem:opens_overEquivalence_mathlib}
2563	  \textit{Topological layer of bridge C; fills the \texttt{Topology/Sheaves/Over.lean} TODO.}
2564	  Symmetrically, the inverse of \(\mathrm{Opens.overEquivalence}\,U\) is cocontinuous from the
2565	  opens topology \(J_U\) of the subspace \(U\) to the \(U\)-slice \(J_X|_U\).
2566	\end{lemma}
2567	\begin{proof}
2568	  \uses{lem:opens_overEquivalence_mathlib}
2569	  As in \cref{lem:overEquivalence_functor_isCocontinuous}, reduce to the pointwise covering
2570	  criterion and the over-sieve image criterion; given a point and a slice-cover member, pull the
2571	  member back along the embedding \(U \hookrightarrow X\) (preimage of an open of \(X\) is an open
2572	  of the subspace) to obtain a cover member of \(J_U\) witnessing the lift.
2573	\end{proof}

2641	\begin{lemma}\leanok
2642	[The Grothendieck-slice restriction equals the geometric restriction (gap1, C)]
2643	  \label{lem:over_restrict_iso}
2644	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.overRestrictIso}
2645	  \uses{lem:modules_restrictFunctor_mathlib, lem:modules_restrictFunctorIsoPullback_mathlib,
2646	    lem:isLocalization_basicOpen_mathlib, def:overEquivalence_sheafCongr,
2647	    lem:overEquivalence_functor_isContinuous, lem:overEquivalence_inverse_isContinuous,
2648	    lem:opens_overEquivalence_mathlib, lem:pushforwardPushforwardEquivalence_mathlib}
2649	  % NOTE: RESOLVED and axiom-clean. AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.overRestrictIso
2650	  % now exists (#print axioms = {propext, Classical.choice, Quot.sound}); gap1 bridge C
2651	  % (steps 1->4) is fully closed. The step-2 geometric ring-sheaf identification collapsed to
2652	  % `rfl` (U.toScheme.ringCatSheaf is definitionally the transport of the sliced sheaf, via
2653	  % toScheme_presheaf_obj/map). The statement is routed through the step-3 equivalence functor:
2654	  % overRestrictIso : (overRestrictEquiv U).functor.obj (M.over U) ≅ (restrictFunctor U.ι).obj M.
2655	  \textit{The one lemma that must touch the Grothendieck slice site; everything downstream of it is
2656	  geometric.}
2657	  Let \(X\) be a scheme and \(U \subseteq X\) an open. The \emph{abstract Grothendieck-slice}
2658	  restriction \(M.\mathrm{over}\,U\) — an object of the category of sheaves of modules over the
2659	  sliced structure sheaf \(\mathcal{O}_X.\mathrm{over}\,U\) on the slice site \(J.\mathrm{over}\,U\) —
2660	  is canonically isomorphic to the \emph{geometric} restriction
2661	  \((\mathrm{restrictFunctor}\,U.\iota).\mathrm{obj}\,M\)
2662	  (\cref{lem:modules_restrictFunctor_mathlib}), equivalently to the pullback \(U.\iota^{*} M\)
2663	  (\cref{lem:modules_restrictFunctorIsoPullback_mathlib}), as a sheaf of modules on the small Zariski
2664	  site of the open subscheme \(U.\mathrm{toScheme}\). The isomorphism is induced by the equivalence
2665	  of sites \(J.\mathrm{over}\,U \simeq \mathrm{Opens}(U.\mathrm{toScheme})\) coming from the opens
2666	  functor of the open immersion \(U.\iota\), under which the sliced sheaf of rings
2667	  \(\mathcal{O}_X.\mathrm{over}\,U\) corresponds to the structure sheaf of \(U.\mathrm{toScheme}\).
2668	  For \(X = \Spec R\) and \(U = D(r)\) a basic open, the target category is
2669	  \((\Spec R_r).\mathrm{Modules}\) under the affine identification \(D(r) \cong \Spec R_r\)
2670	  (\cref{lem:isLocalization_basicOpen_mathlib}).
2671	\end{lemma}
2672	\begin{proof}
2673	  \uses{def:overEquivalence_sheafCongr, lem:overEquivalence_functor_isContinuous,
2674	    lem:overEquivalence_inverse_isContinuous, lem:opens_overEquivalence_mathlib,
2675	    lem:pushforwardPushforwardEquivalence_mathlib, lem:modules_restrictFunctor_mathlib,
2676	    lem:modules_restrictFunctorIsoPullback_mathlib, lem:isLocalization_basicOpen_mathlib}
2677	  The isomorphism is built in four steps, ascending from the purely topological layer to the
2678	  module-level statement.
2679	
2680	  \emph{Step 1 (topological layer --- done).} The opens functor of the open immersion
2681	  \(U.\iota\) realizes the slice of the opens site of \(X\) over \(U\) as the opens site of the
2682	  subspace \(U\); on sheaf categories this is the equivalence
2683	  \(\mathrm{Sheaf}(J_X|_U,\,A) \simeq \mathrm{Sheaf}(J_U,\,A)\) of
2684	  \cref{def:overEquivalence_sheafCongr}, for every target category \(A\). This is the
2685	  topos-theoretic layer, assembled from the cocontinuity/continuity of both legs of
2686	  \(\mathrm{Opens.overEquivalence}\,U\) (\cref{lem:opens_overEquivalence_mathlib}).
2687	
2688	  \emph{Step 2 (geometric ring-sheaf identification --- the current obstacle).} Identify the
2689	  sliced structure sheaf \(\mathcal{O}_X.\mathrm{over}\,U\) with the structure sheaf
2690	  \(\mathcal{O}_{U}\) of the open subscheme \(U.\mathrm{toScheme}\), transported across the
2691	  \(\mathrm{RingCat}\)-valued instance of \cref{def:overEquivalence_sheafCongr}; this is an
2692	  isomorphism of sheaves of rings. The bridge is the factorization of the opens functor of the
2693	  immersion,
2694	  \[
2695	    U.\iota.\mathrm{opensFunctor}
2696	      \;=\; (\mathrm{Opens.overEquivalence}\,U).\mathrm{inverse}
2697	        \;\circ\; \mathrm{Over.forget}\,U ,
2698	  \]
2699	  under which the structure-sheaf comparison reduces to the standard open-immersion
2700	  structure-sheaf identifications; recall that \(\mathrm{restrictFunctor}\,U.\iota\)
2701	  (\cref{lem:modules_restrictFunctor_mathlib}) is precisely pushforward along

2702	  \(U.\iota.\mathrm{opensFunctor}\). This ring-sheaf identification is the remaining geometric
2703	  obstacle.
2704	
2705	  \emph{Step 3 (lift to modules).} Given the step-2 identification of the underlying ring sheaves
2706	  and the continuity of both legs (\cref{lem:overEquivalence_functor_isContinuous},
2707	  \cref{lem:overEquivalence_inverse_isContinuous}), lift the sheaf-of-rings comparison to an
2708	  equivalence of categories of sheaves of modules via
2709	  \(\mathrm{pushforwardPushforwardEquivalence}\)
2710	  (\cref{lem:pushforwardPushforwardEquivalence_mathlib}). Under this equivalence the sliced
2711	  module \(M.\mathrm{over}\,U\) corresponds to the geometric restriction
2712	  \((\mathrm{restrictFunctor}\,U.\iota).\mathrm{obj}\,M\).
2713	
2714	  \emph{Step 4 (compose).} Compose with
2715	  \(\mathrm{restrictFunctorIsoPullback}\) (\cref{lem:modules_restrictFunctorIsoPullback_mathlib})
2716	  to land the isomorphism \(M.\mathrm{over}\,U \cong M.\mathrm{restrict}\,U.\iota \cong
2717	  U.\iota^{*}M\). The two sides live a priori in different module categories, so the literal Lean
2718	  statement of \(\mathrm{overRestrictIso}\) is phrased through the step-3 equivalence functor; for
2719	  \(X = \Spec R\) and \(U = D(r)\) the target is \((\Spec R_r).\mathrm{Modules}\) under
2720	  \(D(r) \cong \Spec R_r\) (\cref{lem:isLocalization_basicOpen_mathlib}).
2721	\end{proof}

2858	[Schematic support of a sheaf of modules]
2859	  \label{def:schematic_support}
2860	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.schematicSupport}
2861	  \uses{def:modules_annihilator, lem:modules_annihilator_ideal_le,
2862	    def:schematic_support_immersion}
2863	  % SOURCE: [Nitsure], \S 1, sub-section "Stratification by Hilbert
2864	  % Polynomials" (read from
2865	  % references/nitsure-hilbert-quot-src/nitsure-hilbert-quot.tex, L468-L471).
2866	  % SOURCE QUOTE:
2867	  % "Let $X\to S$ be a finite type morphism of noetherian schemes,
2868	  %  and let $L$ be a line bundle on $X$. Let $\F$
2869	  %  be any coherent sheaf on
2870	  %  $X$ whose schematic support is proper over $S$."
2871	  \textit{Source: [Nitsure], \S 1 (FGA Explained Ch.~5).}
2872	  Let \(\mathcal{F}\) be a sheaf of modules on a scheme \(X\). The
2873	  \emph{schematic support} of \(\mathcal{F}\) is the closed subscheme of \(X\)
2874	  cut out by its annihilator ideal sheaf (\cref{def:modules_annihilator}):
2875	  \[
2876	    \mathrm{schematicSupport}(\mathcal{F})
2877	    \;=\; V\bigl(\mathrm{Ann}(\mathcal{F})\bigr) \;\hookrightarrow\; X,
2878	  \]
2879	  equipped with its canonical closed immersion into \(X\). This realises
2880	  Nitsure's ``schematic support of \(\mathcal{F}\)'' as a closed subscheme of
2881	  \(X\).
2882	\end{definition}

2884	\begin{definition}\leanok
2885	[Schematic-support immersion]
2886	  \label{def:schematic_support_immersion}
2887	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.schematicSupportι}
2888	  \uses{def:schematic_support, def:modules_annihilator}
2889	  Let \(\mathcal{F}\) be a sheaf of modules on a scheme \(X\). The
2890	  \emph{schematic-support immersion} is the canonical closed immersion
2891	  \[
2892	    \mathrm{schematicSupport}(\mathcal{F}) \;\hookrightarrow\; X
2893	  \]
2894	  of the schematic support (\cref{def:schematic_support}) into the ambient
2895	  scheme, namely the subscheme immersion of the annihilator ideal sheaf
2896	  \(\mathrm{Ann}(\mathcal{F})\) (\cref{def:modules_annihilator}). It is a
2897	  quasi-compact preimmersion onto the support, and it is the morphism whose
2898	  composite with a base morphism \(f : X \to S\) defines proper support
2899	  (\cref{def:has_proper_support}). Proved directly in Lean.
2900	\end{definition}

2902	\begin{lemma}[Properness as a morphism property]
2903	  \label{lem:isProper_mathlib}
2904	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.IsProper}
2905	  \mathlibok
2906	  \textit{Provided by Mathlib
2907	  (\texttt{Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.Morphisms.Proper}).}
2908	  A morphism \(f : X \to S\) of schemes is \emph{proper} iff it is separated,
2909	  universally closed, and locally of finite type. Properness is stable under base
2910	  change: if \(f : X \to S\) is proper and \(g : T \to S\) is any morphism then
2911	  the base change \(X \times_S T \to T\) is proper. Consequently the base-change
2912	  clause that Nitsure invokes (``properness \ldots\ preserved by base-change'')
2913	  applies directly to the proper-support condition below.
2914	\end{lemma}

2916	\begin{definition}

2924	  % $\Quot_{E/X/S}$" (read from
2925	  % references/nitsure-hilbert-quot-src/nitsure-hilbert-quot.tex, L407-L409
2926	  % + L418-L419).
2927	  % SOURCE QUOTE:
2928	  % "(i) a coherent sheaf $\F$ on $X_T = X\times_ST$
2929	  %  such that the schematic support of $\F$ is proper over $T$
2930	  %  and $\F$ is flat over $T$, together with
2931	  %  [...]

2933	  %  by base-change, and as tensor-product is right exact,"
2934	  \textit{Source: [Nitsure], \S 1 (FGA Explained Ch.~5).}
2935	  Let \(\mathcal{F}\) be a sheaf of modules on a scheme \(X\) and let
2936	  \(f : X \to S\) be a morphism. We say \(\mathcal{F}\) \emph{has proper support
2937	  over \(S\) (along \(f\))} when the composite morphism
2938	  \[
2939	    \mathrm{schematicSupport}(\mathcal{F}) \hookrightarrow X \xrightarrow{f} S
2940	  \]
2941	  is proper (\cref{lem:isProper_mathlib}). Since properness is stable under base
2942	  change (\cref{lem:isProper_mathlib}), this condition is preserved by pull-back
2943	  along any \(S'\to S\), which is exactly what the well-definedness of the Quot
2944	  functor's pullback action requires.
2945	\end{definition}
2946	
2947	\begin{definition}
2948	
2949	\leanok
2950	[Locally free of rank \(d\)]
2951	  \label{def:is_locally_free_of_rank}
2952	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.SheafOfModules.IsLocallyFreeOfRank}
2953	  % SOURCE: [Nitsure], \S 1, "Elementary Examples, Exercises (2)" and
2954	  % "Grassmannian of a vector bundle" (read from
2955	  % references/nitsure-hilbert-quot-src/nitsure-hilbert-quot.tex, L562-L564
2956	  % + L584-L585).
2957	  % SOURCE QUOTE:
2958	  % "$\langle \F, q\rangle$ of quotients $q: \oplus^r\,{\mathcal O}_T \to \F$
2959	  %  where $\F$ is a locally free sheaf on $T$ of rank $d$.
2960	  %  [...]
2961	  %  the set of all equivalence classes $\langle \F, q\rangle$ of quotients
2962	  %  $q: E_T \to \F$ where $\F$ is a locally free sheaf on $T$ of rank $d$,"
2963	  \textit{Source: [Nitsure], \S 1, Exercise (2) (FGA Explained Ch.~5).}
2964	  Let \(\mathcal{M}\) be a sheaf of modules on a scheme \(X\) and let
2965	  \(d \in \mathbb{N}\). We say \(\mathcal{M}\) is \emph{locally free of rank
2966	  \(d\)} when there exists an open cover \(\{U_i\}\) of \(X\) together with
2967	  isomorphisms \(\mathcal{M}|_{U_i} \cong \mathcal{O}_{U_i}^{\oplus d}\) for
2968	  each \(i\).
2969	
2970	  \emph{Note.} Mathlib does not provide this predicate.
2971	\end{definition}

801	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — annihilator under localization
802	
803	The annihilator ideal sheaf `def:modules_annihilator` of a coherent sheaf is
804	built from the affine-local data `U ↦ Ann_{O(U)}(F(U))`, packaged as a
805	`Scheme.IdealSheafData`. The structure's coherence field `map_ideal_basicOpen`
806	requires the algebraic fact that, for a *finitely generated* module, the
807	annihilator commutes with localization:
808	`Ann(S⁻¹M) = (Ann M)·S⁻¹R`. Mathlib (at the pinned commit) does not carry this
809	lemma, so it is supplied here as the load-bearing engine for that construction.
810	-/
811	
812	namespace Module
813	
814	/-- For a finitely generated module `M` over a commutative ring `R`, the
815	annihilator commutes with localization: if `Rₚ` localizes `R` at a submonoid
816	`S` and `f : M →ₗ[R] Mₚ` localizes `M` at `S`, then the annihilator of `Mₚ`
817	over `Rₚ` is the extension (`Ideal.map` along `algebraMap R Rₚ`) of the
818	annihilator of `M` over `R`.
819	
820	This is the abstract `IsLocalization`/`IsLocalizedModule` form, matching the
821	shape needed for the affine-basic-open coherence of the annihilator ideal sheaf
822	(`AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.annihilator`, `def:modules_annihilator`):
823	the structure-sheaf restriction `Γ(X,U) → Γ(X, D(f))` is
824	`IsLocalization (powers f)`, and for a quasi-coherent `F` the section
825	restriction is `IsLocalizedModule (powers f)`.
826	
827	Mathlib has no annihilator-localization lemma, so this is project-local. -/
828	theorem annihilator_isLocalizedModule_eq_map
829	    {R : Type*} [CommRing R] (S : Submonoid R)
830	    {Rₚ : Type*} [CommRing Rₚ] [Algebra R Rₚ] [IsLocalization S Rₚ]
831	    {M : Type*} [AddCommGroup M] [Module R M] [Module.Finite R M]
832	    {Mₚ : Type*} [AddCommGroup Mₚ] [Module R Mₚ] [Module Rₚ Mₚ] [IsScalarTower R Rₚ Mₚ]
833	    (f : M →ₗ[R] Mₚ) [IsLocalizedModule S f] :
834	    Module.annihilator Rₚ Mₚ = (Module.annihilator R M).map (algebraMap R Rₚ) := by
835	  classical
836	  obtain ⟨t, htop⟩ := (Module.Finite.fg_top (R := R) (M := M))
837	  -- annihilating a spanning finset suffices for membership in the annihilator
838	  have key : ∀ (r : R), (∀ m ∈ t, r • m = 0) → r ∈ Module.annihilator R M := by
839	    intro r h
840	    rw [Module.mem_annihilator]
841	    intro x
842	    have hx : x ∈ Submodule.span R (t : Set M) := htop ▸ Submodule.mem_top
843	    induction hx using Submodule.span_induction with
844	    | mem y hy => exact h y hy
845	    | zero => simp
846	    | add a b _ _ ha hb => rw [smul_add, ha, hb, add_zero]
847	    | smul c a _ ha => rw [smul_comm, ha, smul_zero]
848	  apply le_antisymm
849	  · -- `Ann Rₚ Mₚ ⊆ (Ann R M).map`: clear one common denominator over the generators
850	    intro y hy
851	    rw [Module.mem_annihilator] at hy
852	    obtain ⟨⟨a, s⟩, rfl⟩ := IsLocalization.mk'_surjective S y
853	    dsimp only at hy ⊢
854	    have hgen : ∀ m ∈ t, ∃ u : S, (u : R) • a • m = 0 := by
855	      intro m hm
856	      have hz := hy (IsLocalizedModule.mk' f m (1 : S))
857	      rw [IsLocalizedModule.mk'_smul_mk' Rₚ f, IsLocalizedModule.mk'_eq_zero,
858	        IsLocalizedModule.eq_zero_iff S f] at hz
859	      obtain ⟨u, hu⟩ := hz
860	      exact ⟨u, hu⟩
861	    choose u hu using hgen
862	    obtain ⟨U, hU⟩ : ∃ U : S, ∀ m ∈ t, (U : R) • a • m = 0 := by
863	      refine ⟨∏ x ∈ t.attach, u x.1 x.2, ?_⟩
864	      intro m hm
865	      obtain ⟨c, hc⟩ :=
866	        Finset.dvd_prod_of_mem (fun x : t => u x.1 x.2) (Finset.mem_attach t ⟨m, hm⟩)
867	      have hcoe : ((∏ x ∈ t.attach, u x.1 x.2 : S) : R) = (u m hm : R) * (c : R) := by
868	        rw [hc]; push_cast; ring
869	      rw [hcoe, mul_smul, smul_comm, hu m hm, smul_zero]
870	    have hUa : (U : R) * a ∈ Module.annihilator R M := by
871	      apply key; intro m hm; rw [mul_smul]; exact hU m hm
872	    have heq : IsLocalization.mk' Rₚ a s
873	        = (algebraMap R Rₚ ((U : R) * a)) * IsLocalization.mk' Rₚ 1 (U * s) := by
874	      rw [← IsLocalization.mk'_eq_mul_mk'_one, IsLocalization.mk'_eq_iff_eq]
875	      push_cast; ring
876	    rw [heq]
877	    exact Ideal.mul_mem_right _ _ (Ideal.mem_map_of_mem _ hUa)
878	  · -- `(Ann R M).map ⊆ Ann Rₚ Mₚ`: the image of an annihilator annihilates
879	    rw [Ideal.map_le_iff_le_comap]
880	    intro a ha
881	    rw [Ideal.mem_comap, Module.mem_annihilator]
882	    rw [Module.mem_annihilator] at ha
883	    intro x
884	    obtain ⟨⟨m, s⟩, rfl⟩ := IsLocalizedModule.mk'_surjective S f x
885	    dsimp only [Function.uncurry]
886	    rw [← IsLocalization.mk'_one (M := S) Rₚ a, IsLocalizedModule.mk'_smul_mk' Rₚ f, ha m,
887	      IsLocalizedModule.mk'_zero]
888	
889	end Module
890	
891	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — quasi-coherent sections localize on a basic open
892	
893	This section builds, bottom-up, toward the keystone
894	`lem:qcoh_section_localization_basicOpen`: for a quasi-coherent sheaf of modules `M`
895	on a scheme `X`, an affine open `U`, and `f ∈ Γ(X,U)`, the restriction
896	`M(U) → M(D(f))` exhibits `M(D(f))` as `IsLocalizedModule (powers f)` over `Γ(X,U)`.
897	
898	The substance is the affine (Spec-local) computation: over `Spec R`, a quasi-coherent
899	sheaf is `Ñ = tilde N` for `N = Γ(M)`, and the basic-open restriction of `Ñ` is the
900	module localization map. Mathlib's `AlgebraicGeometry.tilde` namespace already carries
901	the localization fact for `tilde N` *as the map out of `N`* (the instance
902	`IsLocalizedModule (.powers f) (tilde.toOpen N (basicOpen f)).hom`). The first building
903	block below repackages this as a statement about the *presheaf restriction map* of `Ñ`
904	itself (from global sections to `D(f)`), which is the form the downstream scheme-level
905	argument consumes after the affine identification `M|_U ≅ Ñ`.
906	
907	Mathlib (at the pinned commit) does **not** prove that an arbitrary quasi-coherent sheaf
908	on `Spec R` lies in the essential image of `tilde` (the comment in
909	`Mathlib/AlgebraicGeometry/Modules/Tilde.lean` says this "will later be shown"); the
910	equivalence `QCoh(Spec R) ≃ Mod R` is a genuine gap. Consequently the keystone for an
911	*arbitrary* quasi-coherent `M` is gated on that bridge (`IsQuasicoherent M → IsIso
912	M.fromTildeΓ`); the building blocks here are stated for `tilde N` directly, and for a
913	general `M : (Spec R).Modules` under the explicit hypothesis `[IsIso M.fromTildeΓ]`
914	(equivalently, `M` in the essential image of `tilde`). -/
915	
916	namespace AlgebraicGeometry
917	
918	open CategoryTheory Limits
919	
920	/-- **Basic-open restriction of a `tilde` sheaf is a module localization.**
921	
922	For `N : ModuleCat R` and `f : R`, the presheaf restriction map of the associated sheaf
923	`Ñ = tilde N` from global sections `Γ(Ñ, ⊤)` to the basic open `Γ(Ñ, D(f))` exhibits the
924	latter as `IsLocalizedModule (powers f)` over `R`.
925	
926	This is the affine, Spec-local heart of `lem:qcoh_section_localization_basicOpen`. Mathlib
927	carries the localization fact for the map `tilde.toOpen N (D f) : N → Γ(Ñ, D(f))` out of
928	`N`; since `tilde.toOpen N ⊤ : N → Γ(Ñ, ⊤)` is an isomorphism and
929	`tilde.toOpen N (D f) = tilde.toOpen N ⊤ ≫ restriction` (`tilde.toOpen_res`), precomposing
930	the localization map with the inverse isomorphism (`IsLocalizedModule.of_linearEquiv_right`)
931	transfers the property to the restriction map. Project-local: Mathlib states the fact only
932	for the map out of `N`, not for the presheaf restriction of `Ñ`. -/
933	theorem isLocalizedModule_tilde_restrict {R : CommRingCat.{u}} (N : ModuleCat.{u} R) (f : R) :
934	    IsLocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers f)
935	      ((modulesSpecToSheaf.obj (tilde N)).presheaf.map
936	        (homOfLE (le_top : PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen f ≤ ⊤)).op).hom := by
937	  set res := (modulesSpecToSheaf.obj (tilde N)).presheaf.map
938	        (homOfLE (le_top : PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen f ≤ ⊤)).op with hresdef
939	  have hres := tilde.toOpen_res N ⊤ (PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen f)

958	/-- **Basic-open restriction localizes, for a sheaf in the essential image of `tilde`.**
959	
960	For a sheaf of modules `M` on `Spec R` whose tilde-Gamma counit `M.fromTildeΓ` is an
961	isomorphism (equivalently, `M` lies in the essential image of the `tilde` functor — the
962	honest Spec-affine stand-in for quasi-coherence, see the section header), the presheaf
963	restriction map of `M` from global sections `Γ(M, ⊤)` to the basic open `Γ(M, D(f))`
964	exhibits the latter as `IsLocalizedModule (powers f)` over `R`.
965	
966	This transports `isLocalizedModule_tilde_restrict` across the isomorphism
967	`M.fromTildeΓ : tilde N ⟶ M` (where `N = Γ(M, ⊤)`): the induced presheaf isomorphism is
968	natural in the open, so on each of `⊤` and `D(f)` it provides an `R`-linear isomorphism

1219	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — the over-site ↔ open-subspace sheaf equivalence
1220	
1221	The gap1 slice-to-geometric bridge `lem:over_restrict_iso` (`overRestrictIso`) rests on an
1222	equivalence of *sheaf* categories
1223	`Sheaf ((Opens.grothendieckTopology X).over U) A ≌ Sheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology ↥U) A`
1224	induced by the equivalence of underlying sites
1225	`Opens.overEquivalence U : Over U ≌ Opens ↥U`. Mathlib carries `Opens.overEquivalence` but leaves
1226	the *continuity* of its two functors and the induced sheaf-category equivalence as an explicit TODO
1227	(see `Mathlib/Topology/Sheaves/Over.lean`: "show that both functors of the equivalence
1228	`overEquivalence U` are continuous and induce an equivalence between
1229	`Sheaf ((Opens.grothendieckTopology X).over U) A` and `Sheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology U) A`").
1230	
1231	This section fills that TODO. The two cover-lifting (`IsCocontinuous`) facts are the substance:
1232	a sieve covers in the Grothendieck-topology-over-`U` exactly when its image under the slice
1233	forgetful functor covers in the ambient space, and that condition matches the pointwise covering
1234	condition on the open subspace `↥U` because `Subtype.val : ↥U → X` is an injective open embedding.
1235	From the two cocontinuities, `Equivalence.isDenseSubsite_inverse_of_isCocontinuous` produces the
1236	dense-subsite hypothesis and `Equivalence.sheafCongr` produces the sheaf equivalence.
1237	
1238	It is the foundational (purely topological / topos-theoretic) layer of the slice-to-geometric
1239	transport; the remaining steps toward `overRestrictIso` (identifying the sliced structure sheaf
1240	`O_X.over U` with the open subscheme's structure sheaf `U.toScheme.ringCatSheaf` under this
1241	equivalence, then lifting to sheaves of modules via `pushforwardPushforwardEquivalence` and relating
1242	to `Scheme.Modules.restrictFunctor U.ι`) are the geometric layer above it. -/
1243	
1244	section OverSiteSheafEquivalence
1245	
1246	open TopologicalSpace Topology
1247	
1248	variable {X : Type u} [TopologicalSpace X] (U : Opens X)
1249	
1250	/-- The functor of `Opens.overEquivalence U` is cocontinuous (cover-lifting) from the
1251	`U`-slice of the ambient Grothendieck topology to the Grothendieck topology of the open
1252	subspace `↥U`. Foundational layer of the gap1 slice-to-geometric bridge `overRestrictIso`;
1253	fills the `Mathlib/Topology/Sheaves/Over.lean` TODO. Project-local. -/
1254	instance overEquivalence_functor_isCocontinuous :
1255	    (Opens.overEquivalence U).functor.IsCocontinuous
1256	      ((Opens.grothendieckTopology X).over U) (Opens.grothendieckTopology ↥U) where
1257	  cover_lift := by
1258	    intro Y S hS
1259	    rw [GrothendieckTopology.mem_over_iff]
1260	    intro x hx
1261	    have hxU : x ∈ U := leOfHom Y.hom hx
1262	    have hmem : (⟨x, hxU⟩ : ↥U) ∈ (Opens.overEquivalence U).functor.obj Y := hx
1263	    obtain ⟨V, h, hSh, hxV⟩ := hS ⟨x, hxU⟩ hmem
1264	    have hVle : (V : Set ↥U) ⊆ Subtype.val ⁻¹' (Y.left : Set X) := leOfHom h
1265	    set W : Opens X := ⟨Subtype.val '' (V : Set ↥U),
1266	      (U.isOpenEmbedding'.isOpen_iff_image_isOpen).1 V.isOpen⟩ with hWdef
1267	    have hWle : W ≤ Y.left := by
1268	      intro y hy; obtain ⟨z, hzV, rfl⟩ := hy; exact hVle hzV
1269	    refine ⟨W, homOfLE hWle, ?_, ⟨⟨x, hxU⟩, hxV, rfl⟩⟩
1270	    rw [Sieve.overEquiv_iff]
1271	    change S ((Opens.overEquivalence U).functor.map (Over.homMk (homOfLE hWle)))
1272	    have hdomle :
1273	        (Opens.overEquivalence U).functor.obj (Over.mk ((homOfLE hWle) ≫ Y.hom)) ≤ V := by
1274	      intro z hz
1275	      obtain ⟨z', hz'V, hz'eq⟩ := (hz : (z : X) ∈ (W : Set X))
1276	      exact (Subtype.val_injective hz'eq) ▸ hz'V
1277	    convert S.downward_closed hSh (homOfLE hdomle) using 1
1278	    all_goals apply Subsingleton.elim
1279	
1280	/-- The inverse of `Opens.overEquivalence U` is cocontinuous (cover-lifting) from the
1281	Grothendieck topology of the open subspace `↥U` to the `U`-slice of the ambient Grothendieck
1282	topology. Foundational layer of the gap1 slice-to-geometric bridge `overRestrictIso`;
1283	fills the `Mathlib/Topology/Sheaves/Over.lean` TODO. Project-local. -/
1284	instance overEquivalence_inverse_isCocontinuous :
1285	    (Opens.overEquivalence U).inverse.IsCocontinuous
1286	      (Opens.grothendieckTopology ↥U) ((Opens.grothendieckTopology X).over U) where
1287	  cover_lift := by
1288	    intro W S hS
1289	    rw [GrothendieckTopology.mem_over_iff] at hS
1290	    intro y hy
1291	    have hpy : (y : X) ∈ ((Opens.overEquivalence U).inverse.obj W).left := ⟨y, hy, rfl⟩
1292	    obtain ⟨P, f, hSf0, hpP⟩ := hS (y : X) hpy
1293	    rw [Sieve.overEquiv_iff] at hSf0
1294	    have hPle : (P : Set X) ⊆ ((Opens.overEquivalence U).inverse.obj W).left := leOfHom f
1295	    set V : Opens ↥U :=
1296	      ⟨Subtype.val ⁻¹' (P : Set X), P.isOpen.preimage continuous_subtype_val⟩ with hVdef
1297	    have hVle : V ≤ W := by
1298	      intro z hz
1299	      obtain ⟨z', hz'W, hz'eq⟩ := hPle (hz : (z : X) ∈ (P : Set X))
1300	      exact (Subtype.val_injective hz'eq) ▸ hz'W
1301	    refine ⟨V, homOfLE hVle, ?_, hpP⟩
1302	    change S ((Opens.overEquivalence U).inverse.map (homOfLE hVle))
1303	    have hdomle : ((Opens.overEquivalence U).inverse.obj V).left ≤ P := by
1304	      intro p hp; obtain ⟨p', hp'V, rfl⟩ := hp; exact hp'V
1305	    convert S.downward_closed hSf0 (Over.homMk (homOfLE hdomle) ?_) using 1
1306	    all_goals apply Subsingleton.elim
1307	
1308	/-- The dense-subsite witness for the inverse of `Opens.overEquivalence U`, assembled from the two
1309	cover-lifting facts above. Project-local glue for `overEquivalence_sheafCongr`. -/
1310	instance overEquivalence_inverse_isDenseSubsite :
1311	    (Opens.overEquivalence U).inverse.IsDenseSubsite
1312	      (Opens.grothendieckTopology ↥U) ((Opens.grothendieckTopology X).over U) :=
1313	  Equivalence.isDenseSubsite_inverse_of_isCocontinuous _ _ _
1314	
1315	/-- The functor of `Opens.overEquivalence U` is continuous. Derived from the cocontinuity of the
1316	inverse and the equivalence adjunction `inverse ⊣ functor`. Needed downstream of `overRestrictIso`
1317	for `SheafOfModules.pushforwardPushforwardEquivalence`. Project-local. -/
1318	instance overEquivalence_functor_isContinuous :
1319	    (Opens.overEquivalence U).functor.IsContinuous
1320	      ((Opens.grothendieckTopology X).over U) (Opens.grothendieckTopology ↥U) := by
1321	  haveI : (Opens.overEquivalence U).symm.functor.IsCocontinuous
1322	      (Opens.grothendieckTopology ↥U) ((Opens.grothendieckTopology X).over U) :=
1323	    inferInstanceAs ((Opens.overEquivalence U).inverse.IsCocontinuous _ _)
1324	  exact (Opens.overEquivalence U).symm.toAdjunction.isContinuous_of_isCocontinuous _ _
1325	
1326	/-- The inverse of `Opens.overEquivalence U` is continuous. Derived from the cocontinuity of the
1327	functor and the equivalence adjunction `functor ⊣ inverse`. Needed downstream of `overRestrictIso`
1328	for `SheafOfModules.pushforwardPushforwardEquivalence`. Project-local. -/
1329	instance overEquivalence_inverse_isContinuous :
1330	    (Opens.overEquivalence U).inverse.IsContinuous
1331	      (Opens.grothendieckTopology ↥U) ((Opens.grothendieckTopology X).over U) :=
1332	  (Opens.overEquivalence U).toAdjunction.isContinuous_of_isCocontinuous _ _
1333	
1334	/-- **The over-site ↔ open-subspace sheaf equivalence.** For a topological space `X`, an open
1335	`U ⊆ X`, and any category `A`, the equivalence of sites
1336	`Opens.overEquivalence U : Over U ≌ Opens ↥U`
1337	lifts to an equivalence of sheaf categories
1338	`Sheaf ((Opens.grothendieckTopology X).over U) A ≌ Sheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology ↥U) A`.
1339	
1340	This is exactly the equivalence left as a TODO in `Mathlib/Topology/Sheaves/Over.lean`. It is the
1341	foundational layer of the gap1 slice-to-geometric bridge (`lem:over_restrict_iso`,
1342	`overRestrictIso`): once the sliced structure sheaf `O_X.over U` is identified with the structure
1343	sheaf of the open subscheme `U.toScheme` under this equivalence, a presentation of `M.over U`
1344	transports (via `pushforwardPushforwardEquivalence` + `restrictFunctorIsoPullback`) to a geometric
1345	presentation of `M|_U`. Project-local: Mathlib supplies only the underlying site equivalence. -/
1346	noncomputable def overEquivalence_sheafCongr (A : Type*) [Category A] :
1347	    Sheaf ((Opens.grothendieckTopology X).over U) A ≌ Sheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology ↥U) A :=
1348	  (Opens.overEquivalence U).sheafCongr
1349	    ((Opens.grothendieckTopology X).over U) (Opens.grothendieckTopology ↥U) A
1350	
1351	end OverSiteSheafEquivalence
1352	
1353	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — the slice-to-geometric module bridge (gap1, C)
1354	
1355	This section builds the geometric layer of the gap1 slice-to-geometric bridge
1356	`lem:over_restrict_iso` on top of the topological `overEquivalence_sheafCongr` of the previous
1357	section. It identifies, on the level of *sheaves of modules*, the abstract Grothendieck-slice
1358	restriction `M.over U` (a sheaf of modules over the sliced structure sheaf `O_X.over U` on the slice
1359	site `J.over U`) with the geometric restriction `(restrictFunctor U.ι).obj M` on the small Zariski
1360	site of the open subscheme `U.toScheme`.
1361	
1362	The key structural facts, all holding *definitionally* at the pinned commit, are:
1363	* `(Opens.overEquivalence U).inverse ⋙ Over.forget U = U.ι.opensFunctor` (`rfl`): the inverse leg
1364	  of the site equivalence, post-composed with the slice-forgetful functor, is the opens functor of
1365	  the open immersion `U.ι`;
1366	* consequently `U.toScheme.ringCatSheaf = (overEquivalence_sheafCongr U RingCat).functor.obj
1367	  (X.ringCatSheaf.over U)` (`rfl`): the structure sheaf of the open subscheme is the transport of
1368	  the sliced structure sheaf across the ring-valued sheaf equivalence (this is *step 2*, the
1369	  geometric ring-sheaf identification, discharged by `rfl`).
1370	
1371	From these, `SheafOfModules.pushforwardPushforwardEquivalence` lifts the site equivalence to an
1372	equivalence of categories of sheaves of modules (`overRestrictEquiv`, *step 3*), under whose functor
1373	the sliced module `M.over U` corresponds to the geometric restriction (`overRestrictIso`, *step 4*).
1374	
1375	Mathlib (at the pinned commit) supplies only the underlying site equivalence and the
1376	`pushforward`/`restrictFunctor` machinery; the assembly is project-local. -/
1377	
1378	section OverRestrictBridge
1379	
1380	open TopologicalSpace Topology
1381	
1382	namespace Scheme.Modules
1383	
1384	variable {X : Scheme.{u}} (U : X.Opens)
1385	
1386	/-- **The slice-to-geometric equivalence of categories of sheaves of modules** (gap1, C, step 3).
1387	
1388	For a scheme `X` and an open `U ⊆ X`, the category of sheaves of modules over the sliced structure
1389	sheaf `O_X.over U` on the slice site `J.over U` is equivalent to the category `U.toScheme.Modules`
1390	of sheaves of modules on the open subscheme `U.toScheme`. The equivalence is obtained by lifting the
1391	topological site equivalence `Opens.overEquivalence U` (and its ring-valued sheaf congruence
1392	`overEquivalence_sheafCongr`) to sheaves of modules via
1393	`SheafOfModules.pushforwardPushforwardEquivalence`; the two ring-sheaf comparison morphisms it
1394	consumes are the (co)unit of `Opens.overEquivalence U` whiskered into the structure presheaf, and
1395	the identity (the geometric structure sheaf being *definitionally* the transport of the sliced one).
1396	
1397	Project-local: it is the module-level layer of the gap1 bridge `lem:over_restrict_iso`; Mathlib
1398	supplies only the underlying site equivalence. -/
1399	noncomputable def overRestrictEquiv :
1400	    SheafOfModules.{u} (X.ringCatSheaf.over U) ≌ U.toScheme.Modules :=
1401	  letI eqv := Opens.overEquivalence U
1402	  (SheafOfModules.pushforwardPushforwardEquivalence
1403	      (J := (Opens.grothendieckTopology ↥X).over U) (K := Opens.grothendieckTopology ↥U) eqv
1404	    (S := X.ringCatSheaf.over U) (R := U.toScheme.ringCatSheaf)
1405	    (Sheaf.Hom.mk (Functor.whiskerRight (NatTrans.op eqv.unitIso.inv) (X.ringCatSheaf.over U).obj))
1406	    (Sheaf.Hom.mk (𝟙 _))
1407	    (by ext : 2
1408	        simp only [Sheaf.Hom.mk, Functor.comp_obj, Functor.whiskerLeft_app,
1409	          Functor.whiskerRight_app, NatTrans.op_app, NatTrans.id_app,
1410	          ObjectProperty.homMk_hom, NatTrans.comp_app]
1411	        exact congrArg (Sheaf.over X.ringCatSheaf U).obj.map
1412	          (congrArg Quiver.Hom.op (Equivalence.unitInv_app_inverse eqv _).symm))
1413	    (by ext : 2
1414	        simp only [Sheaf.Hom.mk, Functor.whiskerLeft_app, Functor.whiskerRight_app,
1415	          NatTrans.op_app, ObjectProperty.homMk_hom, NatTrans.comp_app, NatTrans.id_app,
1416	          Functor.comp_obj]
1417	        erw [Category.id_comp, ← Functor.map_comp]
1418	        rename_i x
1419	        have h : (eqv.unitIso.inv.app (Opposite.unop x)).op ≫ (eqv.unit.app (Opposite.unop x)).op
1420	            = 𝟙 _ := by
1421	          rw [← op_comp]
1422	          simp only [CategoryTheory.Equivalence.unit, Iso.hom_inv_id_app, op_id]
1423	        exact (congrArg (Sheaf.over X.ringCatSheaf U).obj.map h).trans
1424	          (CategoryTheory.Functor.map_id _ _))).symm
1425	
1426	/-- **Step-4 functor identification of the gap1 bridge.** The composite of `SheafOfModules.over · U`
1427	with the slice-to-geometric equivalence `overRestrictEquiv U` is the geometric restriction functor
1428	`restrictFunctor U.ι` along the open immersion `U.ι`. Both are pushforwards along the immersion's
1429	opens functor (`(Opens.overEquivalence U).inverse ⋙ Over.forget U = U.ι.opensFunctor`, `rfl`); the
1430	two ring-sheaf comparison morphisms agree, so the identification is `pushforwardComp` followed by
1431	`pushforwardCongr`. Project-local. -/
1432	noncomputable def overRestrictFunctorIso :
1433	    (SheafOfModules.pushforward (S := X.ringCatSheaf.over U) (𝟙 _)) ⋙
1434	        (overRestrictEquiv U).functor ≅ restrictFunctor U.ι :=
1435	  haveI : ((Opens.overEquivalence U).inverse ⋙ Over.forget U).IsContinuous
1436	      (Opens.grothendieckTopology ↥U) (Opens.grothendieckTopology ↥X) :=
1437	    Functor.isContinuous_comp (Opens.overEquivalence U).inverse (Over.forget U)
1438	      (Opens.grothendieckTopology ↥U) ((Opens.grothendieckTopology ↥X).over U)
1439	      (Opens.grothendieckTopology ↥X)
1440	  SheafOfModules.pushforwardComp _ _ ≪≫ SheafOfModules.pushforwardCongr (by cat_disch)
1441	
1442	/-- **The slice-to-geometric isomorphism on an object** (gap1, C, step 4): for a sheaf of modules
1443	`M` on `X`, the transport of the abstract Grothendieck-slice restriction `M.over U` under the
1444	slice-to-geometric equivalence `overRestrictEquiv U` is canonically isomorphic to the geometric
1445	restriction `(restrictFunctor U.ι).obj M`. This is the object-level form of
1446	`overRestrictFunctorIso`; composed with `restrictFunctorIsoPullback` it lands the geometric
1447	restriction as the pullback `U.ι^* M`. Project-local: the load-bearing slice-touching ingredient of
1448	the gap1 transport `lem:over_restrict_iso`. -/
1449	noncomputable def overRestrictIso (M : X.Modules) :
1450	    (overRestrictEquiv U).functor.obj (M.over U) ≅ (restrictFunctor U.ι).obj M :=
1451	  (overRestrictFunctorIso U).app M
1452	
1453	/-- **The slice-to-geometric isomorphism in pullback form** (gap1, C, step 4'): the transport of the
1454	abstract Grothendieck-slice restriction `M.over U` under `overRestrictEquiv U` is canonically
1455	isomorphic to the inverse-image (pullback) `U.ι^* M` of `M` along the open immersion `U.ι`. This is
1456	`overRestrictIso` composed with Mathlib's `restrictFunctorIsoPullback`; it is the form a
1457	presentation of `M.over U` transports into a presentation of the geometric pullback `U.ι^* M`.
1458	Project-local. -/
1459	noncomputable def overRestrictPullbackIso (M : X.Modules) :
1460	    (overRestrictEquiv U).functor.obj (M.over U) ≅ (Scheme.Modules.pullback U.ι).obj M :=
1461	  overRestrictIso U M ≪≫ (restrictFunctorIsoPullback U.ι).app M
1462	
1463	end Scheme.Modules
1464	
1465	end OverRestrictBridge
1466	
1467	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — slice-to-geometric presentation transport (gap1, P1)
1468	
1469	This section builds the geometric milestone of the gap1 per-element transport
1470	`lem:isIso_fromTildeΓ_basicOpen_of_quasicoherent` (P1): a `SheafOfModules.Presentation` of the
1471	abstract Grothendieck-slice restriction `M.over U` is transported, across the slice-to-geometric
1472	bridge `overRestrictPullbackIso` (gap1, C), into a `SheafOfModules.Presentation` of the *geometric*
1473	restriction `(Scheme.Modules.pullback U.ι).obj M = U.ι^* M` on the open subscheme `U.toScheme`.
1474	
1475	The load-bearing ingredient is the unit-iso `overRestrictUnitIso`: the slice-to-geometric
1476	equivalence functor `(overRestrictEquiv U).functor` (definitionally a `SheafOfModules.pushforward`
1477	along the equivalence-of-sites inverse with the *identity* ring comparison) sends the
1478	structure-sheaf module `unit` to `unit`. This is exactly the `F.obj (unit R) ≅ unit S` datum that
1479	`SheafOfModules.Presentation.map` consumes; once it is in hand, `Presentation.map` +

1480	`Presentation.ofIsIso` (across `overRestrictPullbackIso`) realise the transport. The unit-iso rests
1481	on the general fact `isIso_unitToPushforwardObjUnit_of_isIso'`: the canonical map
1482	`unit S ⟶ (pushforward ψ).obj (unit R)` is an iso whenever the ring-sheaf comparison `ψ` is
1483	(here `ψ = 𝟙`).
1484	
1485	Mathlib (at the pinned commit) supplies `SheafOfModules.unitToPushforwardObjUnit` and proves it iso
1486	only under a finality hypothesis on the site functor (`PullbackFree.lean`); the
1487	`IsIso ψ ⟹ IsIso (unitToPushforwardObjUnit ψ)` route used here, and the slice transport, are
1488	project-local. -/
1489	
1490	section SliceGeometricPresentation
1491	
1492	open CategoryTheory Limits TopologicalSpace Topology
1493	
1494	/-- **`unitToPushforwardObjUnit` is an isomorphism when the ring-sheaf comparison is.**
1495	
1496	For a continuous functor `F` of sites and a morphism of ring sheaves
1497	`ψ : S ⟶ (F.sheafPushforwardContinuous …).obj R` that is an isomorphism, the canonical map
1498	`unitToPushforwardObjUnit ψ : unit S ⟶ (pushforward ψ).obj (unit R)` is an isomorphism. Its
1499	component on each object is `(forget₂ RingCat AddCommGrpCat).map (ψ.hom.app _)`, iso as `ψ` is;
1500	the conclusion follows by the reflect-isomorphism functors `SheafOfModules.toSheaf` and
1501	`sheafToPresheaf` together with `NatTrans.isIso_iff_isIso_app`.
1502	
1503	Project-local: Mathlib proves `unitToPushforwardObjUnit` iso only under a finality hypothesis on `F`
1504	(`SheafOfModules.PullbackFree`); this `IsIso ψ`-driven form is the one the slice-to-geometric
1505	unit-iso `overRestrictUnitIso` (gap1, P1) consumes (with `ψ = 𝟙`). -/
1506	private theorem isIso_unitToPushforwardObjUnit_of_isIso' {C : Type u} [Category.{u} C]
1507	    {D : Type u} [Category.{u} D]
1508	    {J : GrothendieckTopology C} {K : GrothendieckTopology D} {Fu : C ⥤ D}
1509	    {S : Sheaf J RingCat.{u}} {Rr : Sheaf K RingCat.{u}} [Fu.IsContinuous J K]
1510	    (ψ : S ⟶ (Fu.sheafPushforwardContinuous RingCat.{u} J K).obj Rr)
1511	    [J.HasSheafCompose (forget₂ RingCat.{u} AddCommGrpCat.{u})]
1512	    [K.HasSheafCompose (forget₂ RingCat.{u} AddCommGrpCat.{u})]
1513	    (hψ : IsIso ψ) :
1514	    IsIso (SheafOfModules.unitToPushforwardObjUnit ψ) := by
1515	  haveI := hψ
1516	  haveI hmap : IsIso ((sheafToPresheaf J RingCat).map ψ) := inferInstance
1517	  rw [NatTrans.isIso_iff_isIso_app] at hmap
1518	  rw [← isIso_iff_of_reflects_iso _ (SheafOfModules.toSheaf _)]
1519	  rw [← isIso_iff_of_reflects_iso _ (sheafToPresheaf _ _)]
1520	  rw [NatTrans.isIso_iff_isIso_app]
1521	  intro V
1522	  haveI hiso : IsIso (ψ.hom.app V) := hmap V
1523	  haveI : IsIso ((forget₂ RingCat AddCommGrpCat).map (ψ.hom.app V)) := inferInstance
1524	  exact this
1525	
1526	namespace Scheme.Modules
1527	
1528	variable {X : Scheme.{u}}
1529	
1530	/-- **The slice-to-geometric equivalence functor sends `unit` to `unit`** (gap1, P1).
1531	
1532	For an open `U ⊆ X`, the functor of the slice-to-geometric equivalence `overRestrictEquiv U`
1533	(definitionally `SheafOfModules.pushforward` along `(Opens.overEquivalence U).inverse` with the
1534	identity ring comparison) carries the sliced structure-sheaf module `unit (O_X.over U)` to the
1535	structure-sheaf module `unit (U.toScheme.ringCatSheaf)` of the open subscheme. This is the
1536	`F.obj (unit R) ≅ unit S` datum consumed by `SheafOfModules.Presentation.map` in
1537	`overRestrictPresentation`. Project-local. -/
1538	noncomputable def overRestrictUnitIso (U : X.Opens) :
1539	    (overRestrictEquiv U).functor.obj (SheafOfModules.unit (X.ringCatSheaf.over U)) ≅
1540	      SheafOfModules.unit U.toScheme.ringCatSheaf := by
1541	  unfold overRestrictEquiv
1542	  try dsimp only [Equivalence.symm_functor]
1543	  refine (@asIso _ _ _ _ (SheafOfModules.unitToPushforwardObjUnit
1544	      (F := (Opens.overEquivalence U).inverse) (J := Opens.grothendieckTopology ↥U)
1545	      (S := U.toScheme.ringCatSheaf) (R := X.ringCatSheaf.over U)
1546	      (ObjectProperty.homMk (𝟙 _)))
1547	    (isIso_unitToPushforwardObjUnit_of_isIso' _ ?hpsi)).symm
1548	  exact inferInstanceAs (IsIso (𝟙 U.toScheme.ringCatSheaf))
1549	
1550	/-- **Slice presentation ⟹ geometric-restriction presentation** (gap1, P1).
1551	
1552	Given a sheaf of modules `M` on `X`, an open `U ⊆ X`, and a `SheafOfModules.Presentation` of the
1553	abstract Grothendieck-slice restriction `M.over U`, there is a `SheafOfModules.Presentation` of the
1554	*geometric* restriction `(pullback U.ι).obj M = U.ι^* M` on the open subscheme `U.toScheme`. The
1555	transport is `Presentation.map` along the slice-to-geometric equivalence functor (using the unit-iso
1556	`overRestrictUnitIso`) followed by `Presentation.ofIsIso` across the bridge
1557	`overRestrictPullbackIso` (gap1, C).
1558	
1559	This closes the slice-touching step of the gap1 per-element transport
1560	`lem:isIso_fromTildeΓ_basicOpen_of_quasicoherent` (P1): with `U = q.X i` and
1561	`P = q.presentation i` it produces a global presentation of `U.ι^* M`; the remaining geometric step
1562	restricts further to a basic affine `D(r) ≅ Spec R_r` and concludes via
1563	`isIso_fromTildeΓ_of_presentation`. Project-local. -/
1564	noncomputable def overRestrictPresentation (U : X.Opens) (M : X.Modules)
1565	    (P : (M.over U).Presentation) : ((Scheme.Modules.pullback U.ι).obj M).Presentation :=
1566	  SheafOfModules.Presentation.ofIsIso.{u} (overRestrictPullbackIso U M).hom
1567	    (SheafOfModules.Presentation.map.{u} P (overRestrictEquiv U).functor (overRestrictUnitIso U))
1568	
1569	set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
1570	set_option maxHeartbeats 2000000 in
1571	set_option synthInstance.maxHeartbeats 800000 in
1572	set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
1573	/-- **Geometric restriction to a cover member is globally presented** (gap1, P1).
1574	
1575	For a sheaf of modules `M` on `X` with quasi-coherence data `q` and an index `i`, the geometric
1576	restriction `(pullback (q.X i).ι).obj M = (q.X i).ι^* M` of `M` to the open subscheme
1577	`(q.X i).toScheme` admits a `SheafOfModules.Presentation`. It is `overRestrictPresentation` applied
1578	to the slice presentation `q.presentation i : (M.over (q.X i)).Presentation` supplied by the
1579	quasi-coherence datum.
1580	
1581	This is the per-cover-member output that feeds the affine descent of the gap1 transport
1582	`lem:isIso_fromTildeΓ_basicOpen_of_quasicoherent` (P1): for `D(r) ≤ q.X i` one further restricts this
1583	presentation to the basic affine `D(r) ≅ Spec R_r` and concludes via
1584	`isIso_fromTildeΓ_of_presentation`. The heartbeat headroom tames the slice-site
1585	`IsRightAdjoint`/`HasSheafify` synthesis blow-up that `Presentation.map` triggers across the
1586	equivalence functor (the same `backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false` incantation Mathlib's own
1587	`QuasicoherentData.bind` uses). Project-local. -/
1588	noncomputable def presentationPullbackιOfQuasicoherentData (M : X.Modules)
1589	    (q : M.QuasicoherentData) (i : q.I) :
1590	    ((Scheme.Modules.pullback (Scheme.Opens.ι (q.X i))).obj M).Presentation :=
1591	  overRestrictPresentation (q.X i) M (q.presentation i)
1592	
1593	end Scheme.Modules
1594	
1595	end SliceGeometricPresentation
1596	
1597	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — basic-open presentation descent (gap1, P1 keystone)
1598	
1599	This section assembles the gap1 per-element keystone
1600	`lem:isIso_fromTildeΓ_basicOpen_of_quasicoherent`
1601	(`isIso_fromTildeΓ_restrict_basicOpen`): on a basic open `D(r)` contained in a cover member
1602	`q.X i` of the quasi-coherence data, the restricted sheaf `M|_{D(r)}` is a geometric tilde, i.e.
1603	its `fromTildeΓ` counit is an isomorphism.
1604	
1605	The route follows the affine descent of the recipe, building on the slice-to-geometric presentation
1606	transport of the previous section:
1607	
1608	1. `presentationPullbackιOfQuasicoherentData M q i` is a *global* `Presentation` of the geometric
1609	   restriction `N := (q.X i).ι^* M` on the open subscheme `Z := (q.X i).toScheme`.
1610	2. For any open `W ⊆ Z`, the global presentation `PN` slices to a slice presentation `N.over W` via
1611	   the single `Presentation.map` of the over-functor `pushforward (𝟙 …)` (the
1612	   `QuasicoherentData.ofPresentation` template — no iterated-slice equivalence is needed because
1613	   `PN` is already a global presentation on the genuine scheme `Z`).
1614	3. `overRestrictPresentation W N PNW` transports it to a global presentation of the geometric
1615	   restriction `(pullback W.ι).obj N` on the open subscheme `W.toScheme`.
1616	4. For `W` *affine*, `IsAffineOpen.isoSpec` identifies `W.toScheme ≅ Spec Γ(Z, W)`; transporting the
1617	   presentation across this iso (whose `Opens.map` is `Final`, so `pullbackObjUnitToUnit` is an iso)
1618	   lands a global presentation on the genuine affine `Spec Γ(Z, W)`.
1619	5. A global presentation forces `fromTildeΓ` to be an isomorphism
1620	   (`isIso_fromTildeΓ_of_presentation`).
1621	
1622	Mathlib (at the pinned commit) carries no `QCoh(Spec R) ≃ Mod R` essential-image bridge; this descent
1623	is project-local. -/
1624	
1625	section BasicOpenPresentationDescent
1626	
1627	open CategoryTheory Limits TopologicalSpace Topology
1628	
1629	namespace Scheme.Modules
1630	
1631	variable {X : Scheme.{u}}
1632	
1633	set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
1634	set_option maxHeartbeats 2000000 in
1635	set_option synthInstance.maxHeartbeats 800000 in
1636	set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
1637	/-- **Presentation of the geometric restriction of `M` to an open `W` of a cover member** (gap1, P1).
1638	
1639	For a sheaf of modules `M` on `X` with quasi-coherence data `q`, an index `i`, and *any* open
1640	`W ⊆ (q.X i).toScheme` of the cover-member subscheme, the geometric restriction
1641	`(pullback W.ι).obj ((pullback (q.X i).ι).obj M)` of `M` (pulled back to `Z := (q.X i).toScheme`,
1642	then to `W`) admits a `SheafOfModules.Presentation` on the open subscheme `W.toScheme`.
1643	
1644	It slices the *global* presentation `presentationPullbackιOfQuasicoherentData M q i` of
1645	`N := (q.X i).ι^* M` on `Z` down to the slice `N.over W` (a single `Presentation.map` of the
1646	over-functor — the `QuasicoherentData.ofPresentation` template, no iterated-slice equivalence
1647	needed since `PN` is global on the genuine scheme `Z`), then geometrizes via
1648	`overRestrictPresentation`. Project-local: feeds the affine descent of the gap1 keystone
1649	`isIso_fromTildeΓ_restrict_basicOpen`. -/
1650	noncomputable def presentationPullbackιRestrict (M : X.Modules)
1651	    (q : M.QuasicoherentData) (i : q.I) (W : (show X.Opens from q.X i).toScheme.Opens) :
1652	    ((Scheme.Modules.pullback (Scheme.Opens.ι W)).obj
1653	      ((Scheme.Modules.pullback (Scheme.Opens.ι (q.X i))).obj M)).Presentation :=
1654	  overRestrictPresentation W ((Scheme.Modules.pullback (Scheme.Opens.ι (q.X i))).obj M)
1655	    (SheafOfModules.Presentation.map.{u}
1656	      (presentationPullbackιOfQuasicoherentData M q i)
1657	      (SheafOfModules.pushforward
1658	        (𝟙 ((show X.Opens from q.X i).toScheme.ringCatSheaf.over W))) (by rfl))
1659	
1660	/-- **The opens functor of an iso of schemes is an equivalence of opens sites.** For `φ : Y ≅ Z`,
1661	the inverse-image functor `Opens.map φ.inv.base : Opens ↥Y ⥤ Opens ↥Z` is an equivalence (with
1662	inverse `Opens.map φ.hom.base`), assembled from the pseudofunctoriality isos `Opens.mapComp` /
1663	`Opens.mapId`. Its purpose is to supply the `Final` instance that makes `pullbackObjUnitToUnit` an
1664	isomorphism in `pullbackSchemeIsoUnitIso`. Project-local. -/
1665	noncomputable def opensMapEquivOfIso {Y Z : Scheme.{u}} (φ : Y ≅ Z) :
1666	    TopologicalSpace.Opens ↥Y ≌ TopologicalSpace.Opens ↥Z where
1667	  functor := Opens.map φ.inv.base
1668	  inverse := Opens.map φ.hom.base
1669	  unitIso := (Opens.mapId _).symm ≪≫
1670	      Opens.mapIso (𝟙 _) (φ.hom.base ≫ φ.inv.base)
1671	        (show (𝟙 _) = φ.hom.base ≫ φ.inv.base by
1672	          rw [← AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Hom.comp_base, φ.hom_inv_id]; rfl) ≪≫
1673	      Opens.mapComp φ.hom.base φ.inv.base
1674	  counitIso := (Opens.mapComp φ.inv.base φ.hom.base).symm ≪≫
1675	      Opens.mapIso (φ.inv.base ≫ φ.hom.base) (𝟙 _)
1676	        (show φ.inv.base ≫ φ.hom.base = 𝟙 _ by
1677	          rw [← AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Hom.comp_base, φ.inv_hom_id]; rfl) ≪≫
1678	      Opens.mapId _
1679	
1680	/-- **The opens functor of an iso of schemes is final.** Immediate from
1681	`opensMapEquivOfIso` (an equivalence is final); the `Final` fact needed by
1682	`pullbackObjUnitToUnit`. Supplied via `haveI` at use sites (instance resolution cannot invert
1683	`φ.inv.base`). Project-local. -/
1684	theorem opensMap_final_of_schemeIso {Y Z : Scheme.{u}} (φ : Y ≅ Z) :
1685	    (Opens.map φ.inv.base).Final := by
1686	  haveI : (Opens.map φ.inv.base).IsEquivalence := (opensMapEquivOfIso φ).isEquivalence_functor
1687	  infer_instance
1688	
1689	/-- **Pullback along an iso of schemes sends the unit module to the unit module** (gap1, P1).
1690	
1691	For an isomorphism of schemes `φ : Y ≅ Z`, the pullback functor along `φ.inv : Z ⟶ Y` carries the
1692	structure-sheaf (unit) module of `Y` to that of `Z`. The underlying canonical comparison
1693	`pullbackObjUnitToUnit` is an isomorphism because the site functor `Opens.map φ.inv.base` of an iso
1694	of schemes is `Final` (`opensMap_final_of_schemeIso`). This is the `F.obj (unit R) ≅ unit S` datum
1695	consumed by `Presentation.map` along `pullback φ.inv` in `presentationPullbackOfSchemeIso`.
1696	Project-local. -/
1697	noncomputable def pullbackSchemeIsoUnitIso {Y Z : Scheme.{u}} (φ : Y ≅ Z) :
1698	    (SheafOfModules.pullback (φ.inv.toRingCatSheafHom)).obj (SheafOfModules.unit Y.ringCatSheaf) ≅
1699	      SheafOfModules.unit Z.ringCatSheaf := by
1700	  haveI : (Opens.map φ.inv.base).Final := opensMap_final_of_schemeIso φ
1701	  haveI : (SheafOfModules.pushforward (φ.inv.toRingCatSheafHom)).IsRightAdjoint := inferInstance
1702	  exact asIso (SheafOfModules.pullbackObjUnitToUnit (φ.inv.toRingCatSheafHom))
1703	
1704	set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
1705	set_option maxHeartbeats 2000000 in
1706	set_option synthInstance.maxHeartbeats 800000 in
1707	set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
1708	/-- **A presentation transports across the pullback by an iso of schemes** (gap1, P1, step 4).
1709	
1710	Given an isomorphism of schemes `φ : Y ≅ Z` and a `SheafOfModules.Presentation` of a module `N` on
1711	`Y`, the geometric pullback `(pullback φ.inv).obj N` of `N` to `Z` admits a presentation. It is
1712	`Presentation.map` along the colimit-preserving pullback functor `pullback φ.inv`, using the unit-iso
1713	`pullbackSchemeIsoUnitIso φ`. This is the affine-identification transport step of the gap1 keystone:
1714	applied with `φ` the `IsAffineOpen.isoSpec` of the affine restriction, it moves the presentation onto
1715	a genuine `Spec`. Project-local. -/
1716	noncomputable def presentationPullbackOfSchemeIso {Y Z : Scheme.{u}} (φ : Y ≅ Z)
1717	    (N : Y.Modules) (P : N.Presentation) :
1718	    ((Scheme.Modules.pullback φ.inv).obj N).Presentation :=
1719	  haveI : PreservesColimitsOfSize.{u, u, u, u, u + 1, u + 1} (Scheme.Modules.pullback φ.inv) :=
1720	    (Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction φ.inv).leftAdjoint_preservesColimits
1721	  SheafOfModules.Presentation.map.{u} P (Scheme.Modules.pullback φ.inv)
1722	    (pullbackSchemeIsoUnitIso φ).symm
1723	
1724	set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
1725	set_option maxHeartbeats 2000000 in
1726	set_option synthInstance.maxHeartbeats 800000 in
1727	set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
1728	/-- **Quasi-coherent restricts to a tilde on every affine open of a cover member** (gap1, P1).
1729	
1730	For a sheaf of modules `M` on `X` with quasi-coherence data `q`, an index `i`, and an *affine* open
1731	`W ⊆ (q.X i).toScheme` of the cover-member subscheme, the geometric restriction of `M` to the affine
1732	`Spec Γ((q.X i).toScheme, W) ≅ W` (pulled back to `Z := (q.X i).toScheme`, then to `W`, then across
1733	the affine identification `IsAffineOpen.isoSpec`) has an isomorphism `fromTildeΓ` counit — i.e. it is
1734	a geometric tilde.
1735	
1736	This is the geometric heart of the gap1 per-element transport: the slice presentation supplied by
1737	the quasi-coherence datum geometrizes (`presentationPullbackιRestrict`) to a global presentation on
1738	`W.toScheme`, which transports across the affine iso (`presentationPullbackOfSchemeIso`) to a global
1739	presentation on the genuine affine `Spec Γ(Z, W)`; a global presentation forces `fromTildeΓ` to be an
1740	isomorphism (`isIso_fromTildeΓ_of_presentation`). Project-local: Mathlib has no
1741	`QCoh(Spec R) ≃ Mod R` essential-image bridge. -/
1742	theorem isIso_fromTildeΓ_presentationPullback (M : X.Modules)
1743	    (q : M.QuasicoherentData) (i : q.I)
1744	    (W : (show X.Opens from q.X i).toScheme.Opens) (hW : IsAffineOpen W) :
1745	    IsIso ((Scheme.Modules.pullback hW.isoSpec.inv).obj
1746	      ((Scheme.Modules.pullback (Scheme.Opens.ι W)).obj
1747	        ((Scheme.Modules.pullback (Scheme.Opens.ι (q.X i))).obj M))).fromTildeΓ :=
1748	  isIso_fromTildeΓ_of_presentation _
1749	    (presentationPullbackOfSchemeIso hW.isoSpec
1750	      ((Scheme.Modules.pullback (Scheme.Opens.ι W)).obj
1751	        ((Scheme.Modules.pullback (Scheme.Opens.ι (q.X i))).obj M))
1752	      (presentationPullbackιRestrict M q i W))
1753	
1754	set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
1755	set_option maxHeartbeats 2000000 in
1756	set_option synthInstance.maxHeartbeats 800000 in
1757	set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
1758	/-- **Quasi-coherent restricts to a tilde on each basic open of the cover** (gap1, P1 keystone,
1759	`lem:isIso_fromTildeΓ_basicOpen_of_quasicoherent`).
1760	
1761	Let `M` be a sheaf of modules on `Spec R` with quasi-coherence data `q`, and let `r : R` with
1762	`D(r) ≤ q.X i` for some cover member. Then the geometric restriction of `M` to the affine basic open
1763	`D(r)` — realised as the preimage `W := (q.X i).ι ⁻¹ᵁ D(r)` inside the cover-member subscheme
1764	`Z := (q.X i).toScheme`, transported across the affine identification `W ≅ Spec Γ(Z, W)` (which is
1765	`Spec R_r` since `D(r)` is affine) — has an isomorphism `fromTildeΓ` counit, i.e. `M|_{D(r)}` is a
1766	geometric tilde.
1767	
1768	This is the per-element step of gap1: it is the affine instance `W = (q.X i).ι ⁻¹ᵁ D(r)` of
1769	`isIso_fromTildeΓ_presentationPullback`, with affineness of `W` from
1770	`IsAffineOpen.Spec_basicOpen` (`D(r)` is affine in `Spec R`) and
1771	`IsAffineOpen.preimage_of_isOpenImmersion` (its preimage under the open immersion `(q.X i).ι` is
1772	affine, using `D(r) ≤ q.X i = (q.X i).ι.opensRange`). Project-local. -/
1773	theorem isIso_fromTildeΓ_restrict_basicOpen {R : CommRingCat.{u}}
1774	    (M : (Spec R).Modules) (q : M.QuasicoherentData) (r : R) (i : q.I)
1775	    (hr : (PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen r : (Spec R).Opens) ≤ q.X i) :
1776	    IsIso (@Scheme.Modules.fromTildeΓ
1777	      (Γ(↑(q.X i), (Scheme.Opens.ι (q.X i)) ⁻¹ᵁ (PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen r)))
1778	      ((Scheme.Modules.pullback
1779	          (((IsAffineOpen.Spec_basicOpen r).preimage_of_isOpenImmersion (Scheme.Opens.ι (q.X i))
1780	            (by rw [Scheme.Opens.opensRange_ι]; exact hr)).isoSpec.inv)).obj
1781	        ((Scheme.Modules.pullback (Scheme.Opens.ι
1782	            ((Scheme.Opens.ι (q.X i)) ⁻¹ᵁ (PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen r)))).obj
1783	          ((Scheme.Modules.pullback (Scheme.Opens.ι (q.X i))).obj M)))) :=
1784	  isIso_fromTildeΓ_presentationPullback M q i
1785	    ((Scheme.Opens.ι (q.X i)) ⁻¹ᵁ (PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen r))
1786	    ((IsAffineOpen.Spec_basicOpen r).preimage_of_isOpenImmersion (Scheme.Opens.ι (q.X i))
1787	      (by rw [Scheme.Opens.opensRange_ι]; exact hr))
1788	
1789	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — gap1-D: the section-localization descent
1790	
1791	The keystone `isLocalizedModule_basicOpen_descent` reduces (Hartshorne II.5.3 / Stacks
1792	`lemma-invert-f-sections`) to a finite-cover sheaf-gluing argument.  The single geometric input
1793	is the **per-cover-element** fact that on each `D(r)` of a finite cover `{D(r_j)}` of `Spec R`
1794	(with `D(r_j) ≤ q.X i`), the basic-open restriction `Γ(M, D(r)) → Γ(M, D(f) ⊓ D(r))` is a
1795	localization at `powers f` — this is exactly the P1 local-tilde data transported to sections, and
1796	is the gated hypothesis `Hfr` below.  Given `Hfr` (for every `r` whose `D(r)` sits inside a cover
1797	member, hence also for the overlaps `D(r r')`), the descent is pure sheaf theory: separatedness
1798	gives the `exists_of_eq` field, gluing the patched compatible family gives `surj'`, and the global
1799	`map_units` field holds for arbitrary `M` (`map_units_restrict_basicOpen`). -/
1800	
1801	/-- A finite family `t` spanning `R` gives a basic-open cover of `Spec R`: the supremum of the
1802	`D(r)` over `r ∈ t` is `⊤`.  Project-local glue feeding the sheaf-gluing reduction of the
1803	section-localization descent. -/
1804	private lemma iSup_basicOpen_subtype_eq_top {R : CommRingCat.{u}} {t : Finset R}
1805	    (hspan : Ideal.span (t : Set R) = ⊤) :
1806	    (⨆ r : {x // x ∈ t}, (PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen (r : R) : (Spec R).Opens)) = ⊤ := by
1807	  rw [iSup_subtype]
1808	  have h := (PrimeSpectrum.iSup_basicOpen_eq_top_iff' (s := (t : Set R))).mpr hspan
1809	  simpa using h
1810	
1811	/-- Restriction maps of `modulesSpecToSheaf.obj M` compose: restricting `A → B → C` equals the
1812	direct restriction `A → C`.  Poset-hom uniqueness makes the two intermediate morphisms compose to
1813	the direct one.  Project-local bookkeeping for the section-localization descent. -/
1814	private lemma res_comp {R : CommRingCat.{u}}
1815	    (F : TopCat.Sheaf (ModuleCat.{u} ↑R) ↑(Spec R).toPresheafedSpace)
1816	    {A B C : (Spec R).Opens} (hBA : B ≤ A) (hCB : C ≤ B) (hCA : C ≤ A)
1817	    (y : ToType (F.presheaf.obj (.op A))) :
1818	    (F.presheaf.map (homOfLE hCB).op).hom ((F.presheaf.map (homOfLE hBA).op).hom y)
1819	      = (F.presheaf.map (homOfLE hCA).op).hom y := by

2040	  -- Stage 5: x|_{D(f)} = f^(N+P) • y, by separatedness over the cover {D(f) ⊓ D(r)} of D(f).
2041	  refine ⟨x, P + N, ?_⟩
2042	  have hcoverDf : (PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen f : (Spec R).Opens)
2043	      ≤ ⨆ r : {x // x ∈ t}, (PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen f ⊓ PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen (r:R)) := by
2044	    rw [← inf_iSup_eq, iSup_basicOpen_subtype_eq_top hspan, inf_top_eq]
2045	  refine TopCat.Sheaf.eq_of_locally_eq' (modulesSpecToSheaf.obj M)
2046	    (fun r : {x // x ∈ t} => (PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen f ⊓ PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen (r:R)))
2047	    (PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen f) (fun r => homOfLE inf_le_left) hcoverDf
2048	    (f ^ (P + N) • y)
2049	    (((modulesSpecToSheaf.obj M).presheaf.map
2050	      (homOfLE (le_top : PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen f ≤ ⊤)).op).hom x) ?_
2051	  intro r
2052	  -- LHS = f^(N+P) • (y|_{D(f) ⊓ D(r)})
2053	  change ((modulesSpecToSheaf.obj M).presheaf.map
2054	      (homOfLE (inf_le_left : PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen f ⊓ PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen (r:R)
2055	        ≤ PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen f)).op).hom (f ^ (P + N) • y)
2056	    = ((modulesSpecToSheaf.obj M).presheaf.map
2057	      (homOfLE (inf_le_left : PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen f ⊓ PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen (r:R)
2058	        ≤ PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen f)).op).hom
2059	      (((modulesSpecToSheaf.obj M).presheaf.map
2060	        (homOfLE (le_top : PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen f ≤ ⊤)).op).hom x)
2061	  -- compute the right-hand side via x|_{D(r)} = f^P • b r
2062	  have ex := res_comp (modulesSpecToSheaf.obj M) (A := ⊤) (B := PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen f)
2063	      (C := PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen f ⊓ PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen (r:R))
2064	      le_top inf_le_left le_top x
2065	  have ex2 := res_comp (modulesSpecToSheaf.obj M) (A := ⊤) (B := PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen (r:R))
2066	      (C := PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen f ⊓ PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen (r:R))
2067	      le_top inf_le_right le_top x
2068	  have hxr : ((modulesSpecToSheaf.obj M).presheaf.map
2069	      (homOfLE (le_top : PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen (r:R) ≤ ⊤)).op).hom x
2070	      = f ^ P • (f ^ (N - m r) • a r) := hx r
2071	  -- ρ[D(f),Dfr] (x|_{D(f)}) = ρ[⊤,Dfr] x = ρ[D(r),Dfr] (x|_{D(r)}) = ρ[D(r),Dfr] (f^P • b r)
2072	  have hRHS : ((modulesSpecToSheaf.obj M).presheaf.map
2073	      (homOfLE (inf_le_left : PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen f ⊓ PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen (r:R)
2074	        ≤ PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen f)).op).hom
2075	      (((modulesSpecToSheaf.obj M).presheaf.map
2076	        (homOfLE (le_top : PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen f ≤ ⊤)).op).hom x)
2077	    = f ^ (P + N) • (((modulesSpecToSheaf.obj M).presheaf.map
2078	        (homOfLE (inf_le_left : PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen f ⊓ PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen (r:R)
2079	          ≤ PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen f)).op).hom y) :=
2080	    ex.trans (ex2.symm.trans ((congrArg _ hxr).trans
2081	      ((LinearMap.map_smul _ (f ^ P) (f ^ (N - m r) • a r)).trans
2082	        ((congrArg (f ^ P • ·) (hb r)).trans
2083	          ((mul_smul (f ^ P) (f ^ N) _).symm.trans (congrArg (· • _) (pow_add f P N).symm))))))
2084	  exact (LinearMap.map_smul _ (f ^ (P + N)) y).trans hRHS.symm
2085	
2086	/-- **Section-localization descent from a local-tilde cover (gap1 keystone, D), cover form.**  Let
2087	`M` be a sheaf of modules on `Spec R` and `f : R`.  Suppose `{D(r)}_{r ∈ t}` is a finite basic-open
2088	cover of `Spec R` (`Ideal.span t = ⊤`) and for every open `U` contained in some cover member `D(r)`
2089	(in particular each `D(r)` and each overlap `D(r) ⊓ D(r')`) the basic-open restriction
2090	`Γ(M, U) → Γ(M, D(f) ⊓ U)` is a localization at `powers f` (`Hfr` — the gated P1 local-tilde data).
2091	Then the section restriction `Γ(M, ⊤) → Γ(M, D(f))` is `IsLocalizedModule (powers f)` over `R`.
2092	
2093	This is the project-internal finite-equalizer/flatness descent of Stacks `lemma-invert-f-sections`
2094	(Hartshorne II.5.3), built without the global affine `QCoh(Spec R) ≃ Mod R` equivalence (which is
2095	gap1 itself): `map_units` holds for arbitrary `M` (`map_units_restrict_basicOpen`), `surj'` is
2096	`descent_surj`, and `exists_of_eq` is `descent_smul_eq_zero`.  The named gap1 keystone
2097	`isLocalizedModule_basicOpen_descent` for quasi-coherent `M` is this lemma instantiated at the cover
2098	`exists_finite_basicOpen_cover_le_quasicoherentData` once `Hfr` is produced from
2099	`isIso_fromTildeΓ_restrict_basicOpen` (the slice→Spec-`R_r` section transport, the remaining gated
2100	step).  Project-local. -/
2101	theorem isLocalizedModule_basicOpen_descent_of_cover {R : CommRingCat.{u}} (M : (Spec R).Modules)
2102	    (f : R) (t : Finset R) (hspan : Ideal.span (t : Set R) = ⊤)
2103	    (Hfr : ∀ U : (Spec R).Opens, (∃ r ∈ t, U ≤ PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen r) →
2104	      IsLocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers f)
2105	        ((modulesSpecToSheaf.obj M).presheaf.map
2106	          (homOfLE (inf_le_right : PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen f ⊓ U ≤ U)).op).hom) :
2107	    IsLocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers f)
2108	      ((modulesSpecToSheaf.obj M).presheaf.map
2109	        (homOfLE (le_top : PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen f ≤ ⊤)).op).hom where
2110	  map_units := map_units_restrict_basicOpen M f
2111	  surj y := by
2112	    obtain ⟨x, n, hxn⟩ := descent_surj M f t hspan (fun U _ hcov => Hfr U hcov) y
2113	    exact ⟨⟨x, ⟨f ^ n, n, rfl⟩⟩, hxn⟩
2114	  exists_of_eq {x₁ x₂} h := by
2115	    have hθ : ((modulesSpecToSheaf.obj M).presheaf.map
2116	        (homOfLE (le_top : PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen f ≤ ⊤)).op).hom (x₁ - x₂) = 0 := by
2117	      rw [map_sub, h, sub_self]
