2163	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — `IsLocalizedModule` transport for gap1-D Hfr
2164	
2165	The section-transport iso `gammaPullbackImageIso` is only an additive-group (`Ab`) isomorphism,
2166	semilinear over the *source-scheme* section ring, whereas the `Hfr` hypothesis of
2167	`isLocalizedModule_basicOpen_descent_of_cover` is an `IsLocalizedModule` statement `R`-linear over
2168	the base ring `R`. Two Mathlib-absent transport ingredients bridge the gap:
2169	
2170	* **(I)** `isLocalizedModule_of_ringEquiv_semilinear` — transport `IsLocalizedModule S g` across a
2171	  ring-iso-`σ`-semilinear `AddEquiv` pair. Mathlib only has the same-ring `of_linearEquiv` /
2172	  `of_linearEquiv_right`; the section iso crosses a ring iso, so this is the genuine gap.
2173	* **(II)** `isLocalizedModule_restrictScalars_powers_algebraMap` — a localization at
2174	  `powers (algebraMap R Rr f)` over a base-changed ring `Rr` (here `R` localized at `r`) is, after
2175	  restriction of scalars, a localization at `powers f` over `R`.

2178	
2179	/-- **(I) Ring-iso-semilinear `IsLocalizedModule` transport.** Given a ring isomorphism
2180	`σ : R ≃+* R'`, two `σ`-semilinear additive isomorphisms `e₁ : M₁ ≃+ N₁`, `e₂ : M₂ ≃+ N₂` (i.e.
2181	`eᵢ (a • x) = σ a • eᵢ x`), and an `R'`-linear map `h : N₁ →ₗ[R'] N₂` intertwining a localization
2182	map `g` with the `eᵢ` (`h (e₁ x) = e₂ (g x)`), the map `h` is a localization at the image submonoid
2183	`S.map σ`. Mathlib only provides the same-ring `IsLocalizedModule.of_linearEquiv`; this crosses a
2184	ring iso, the form needed to turn the `Ab`/semilinear section iso `gammaPullbackImageIso` into the
2185	`R`-linear `Hfr` data. Project-local. -/
2186	theorem isLocalizedModule_of_ringEquiv_semilinear
2187	    {R R' : Type*} [CommRing R] [CommRing R'] (σ : R ≃+* R')
2188	    {M₁ M₂ N₁ N₂ : Type*}
2189	    [AddCommGroup M₁] [Module R M₁] [AddCommGroup M₂] [Module R M₂]
2190	    [AddCommGroup N₁] [Module R' N₁] [AddCommGroup N₂] [Module R' N₂]
2191	    (S : Submonoid R)
2192	    (g : M₁ →ₗ[R] M₂) [IsLocalizedModule S g]
2193	    (e₁ : M₁ ≃+ N₁) (e₂ : M₂ ≃+ N₂)
2194	    (he₁ : ∀ (a : R) (x : M₁), e₁ (a • x) = σ a • e₁ x)
2195	    (he₂ : ∀ (a : R) (x : M₂), e₂ (a • x) = σ a • e₂ x)
2196	    (h : N₁ →ₗ[R'] N₂)
2197	    (hh : ∀ x, h (e₁ x) = e₂ (g x)) :
2198	    IsLocalizedModule (S.map (σ : R →+* R')) h where
2199	  map_units x := by
2200	    obtain ⟨s, hs, hsx⟩ := x.2
2201	    rw [Module.End.isUnit_iff]
2202	    have hsrc := IsLocalizedModule.map_units g ⟨s, hs⟩
2203	    rw [Module.End.isUnit_iff] at hsrc
2204	    have hfun : (⇑(algebraMap R' (Module.End R' N₂) (↑x : R')))
2205	        = ⇑e₂ ∘ ⇑(algebraMap R (Module.End R M₂) (⟨s, hs⟩ : S)) ∘ ⇑e₂.symm := by
2206	      funext y
2207	      rw [Module.algebraMap_end_apply, Function.comp_apply, Function.comp_apply,
2208	        Module.algebraMap_end_apply, he₂, e₂.apply_symm_apply]
2209	      congr 1
2210	      exact hsx.symm
2211	    rw [hfun]
2212	    exact e₂.bijective.comp (hsrc.comp e₂.symm.bijective)
2213	  surj y := by
2214	    obtain ⟨⟨x, s⟩, hx⟩ := IsLocalizedModule.surj S g (e₂.symm y)
2215	    refine ⟨⟨e₁ x, ⟨σ ↑s, ↑s, s.2, rfl⟩⟩, ?_⟩
2216	    have he : e₂ ((↑s : R) • e₂.symm y) = e₂ (g x) := congrArg e₂ hx
2217	    rw [he₂, e₂.apply_symm_apply, ← hh] at he
2218	    exact he
2219	  exists_of_eq {y₁ y₂} heq := by
2220	    have h1 : e₂ (g (e₁.symm y₁)) = e₂ (g (e₁.symm y₂)) := by
2221	      rw [← hh, ← hh, e₁.apply_symm_apply, e₁.apply_symm_apply]; exact heq
2222	    obtain ⟨c, hc⟩ := IsLocalizedModule.exists_of_eq (S := S) (f := g) (e₂.injective h1)
2223	    refine ⟨⟨σ ↑c, ↑c, c.2, rfl⟩, ?_⟩
2224	    have hc' : (↑c : R) • e₁.symm y₁ = (↑c : R) • e₁.symm y₂ := hc
2225	    have hcc := congrArg e₁ hc'
2226	    rw [he₁, he₁, e₁.apply_symm_apply, e₁.apply_symm_apply] at hcc
2227	    exact hcc
2228	
2229	/-- **(II) Localization at a base-changed submonoid descends to the base ring.** If `g` is
2230	`Rr`-linear and exhibits a localization at `powers (algebraMap R Rr f)` over a base-changed ring
2231	`Rr` (an algebra over `R` — here `R` localized at some `r`), then its restriction of scalars to `R`
2232	is a localization
2233	at `powers f` over `R`. This lets the `R_r`-level localization that P1 (`IsIso fromTildeΓ`) produces
2234	on the slice `Spec R_r` be read back as the `R`-level `Hfr` data the cover-form descent consumes.
2235	Mathlib-absent; project-local. -/
2236	theorem isLocalizedModule_restrictScalars_powers_algebraMap
2237	    {R Rr : Type*} [CommRing R] [CommRing Rr] [Algebra R Rr]
2238	    {M₁ M₂ : Type*} [AddCommGroup M₁] [Module R M₁] [Module Rr M₁] [IsScalarTower R Rr M₁]
2239	    [AddCommGroup M₂] [Module R M₂] [Module Rr M₂] [IsScalarTower R Rr M₂]

2240	    (f : R) (g : M₁ →ₗ[Rr] M₂)
2241	    [IsLocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers (algebraMap R Rr f)) g] :
2242	    IsLocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers f) (g.restrictScalars R) where
2243	  map_units x := by
2244	    obtain ⟨n, hn⟩ := x.2
2245	    have hn' : f ^ n = ↑x := hn
2246	    rw [Module.End.isUnit_iff]
2247	    have hmem : (algebraMap R Rr f) ^ n ∈ Submonoid.powers (algebraMap R Rr f) := ⟨n, rfl⟩
2248	    have hsrc := IsLocalizedModule.map_units g ⟨_, hmem⟩
2249	    rw [Module.End.isUnit_iff] at hsrc
2250	    have hfun : ⇑(algebraMap R (Module.End R M₂) (↑x : R))
2251	        = ⇑(algebraMap Rr (Module.End Rr M₂) ((algebraMap R Rr f) ^ n)) := by
2252	      funext z
2253	      rw [Module.algebraMap_end_apply, Module.algebraMap_end_apply, ← hn', ← map_pow,
2254	        algebraMap_smul]
2255	    rw [hfun]; exact hsrc
2256	  surj y := by
2257	    obtain ⟨⟨x, s⟩, hx⟩ := IsLocalizedModule.surj (Submonoid.powers (algebraMap R Rr f)) g y
2258	    obtain ⟨n, hn⟩ := s.2
2259	    have hn' : (algebraMap R Rr f) ^ n = ↑s := hn
2260	    refine ⟨⟨x, ⟨f ^ n, n, rfl⟩⟩, ?_⟩
2261	    have hsmul : (f ^ n : R) • y = (↑s : Rr) • y := by
2262	      rw [← hn', ← map_pow, algebraMap_smul]
2263	    change (f ^ n : R) • y = (g.restrictScalars R) x
2264	    rw [hsmul, LinearMap.coe_restrictScalars]; exact hx
2265	  exists_of_eq {x₁ x₂} heq := by
2266	    obtain ⟨c, hc⟩ := IsLocalizedModule.exists_of_eq (S := Submonoid.powers (algebraMap R Rr f))
2267	      (f := g) heq
2268	    obtain ⟨n, hn⟩ := c.2
2269	    have hn' : (algebraMap R Rr f) ^ n = ↑c := hn
2270	    refine ⟨⟨f ^ n, n, rfl⟩, ?_⟩
2271	    have e1 : (f ^ n : R) • x₁ = (↑c : Rr) • x₁ := by rw [← hn', ← map_pow, algebraMap_smul]
2272	    have e2 : (f ^ n : R) • x₂ = (↑c : Rr) • x₂ := by rw [← hn', ← map_pow, algebraMap_smul]
2273	    change (f ^ n : R) • x₁ = (f ^ n : R) • x₂
2274	    rw [e1, e2]; exact hc

2276	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — gap1-D Hfr: pullback-section transport
2277	
2278	The remaining gated ingredient of the named gap1 keystone
2279	`isLocalizedModule_basicOpen_descent` is the **section-level** analogue of P1's object-level
2280	transport: for an open immersion `f : X ⟶ Y` and a sheaf of modules `M` on `Y`, the sections of the
2281	geometric pullback `(pullback f).obj M` over an open `U ⊆ X` are canonically identified with the
2282	sections of `M` over the image `f ''ᵁ U`.
2283	
2284	The construction is `Γ(-, U)` applied to the inverse of Mathlib's `restrictFunctorIsoPullback f`
2285	(`restrictFunctor f ≅ pullback f`) at `M`, using the *definitional* identity
2286	`Γ((restrictFunctor f).obj M, U) = Γ(M, f ''ᵁ U)` (`Scheme.Modules.restrict_obj`, `rfl`). Because
2287	both sides are `Γ(-, U)` of a single fixed module isomorphism, naturality in `U` (intertwining the
2288	presheaf restriction maps) is free: it is the naturality of the underlying abelian-presheaf
2289	morphism. Mathlib-absent at the pinned commit; project-bespoke. -/
2290	
2291	/-- **Global sections of a pullback along an open immersion are sections over the image**
2292	(gap1, Hfr section transport, general open). For an open immersion `f : X ⟶ Y`, a sheaf of modules
2293	`M` on `Y`, and an open `U ⊆ X`, the additive groups of sections satisfy
2294	`Γ((pullback f).obj M, U) ≅ Γ(M, f ''ᵁ U)`. This is `Γ(-, U)` of `(restrictFunctorIsoPullback f)⁻¹`
2295	at `M`; the codomain is `Γ((restrictFunctor f).obj M, U) = Γ(M, f ''ᵁ U)` definitionally. Naturality
2296	in `U` is `gammaPullbackImageIso_hom_naturality`. -/
2297	noncomputable def gammaPullbackImageIso {X Y : Scheme.{u}} (f : X ⟶ Y) [IsOpenImmersion f]
2298	    (M : Y.Modules) (U : X.Opens) :
2299	    Γ((Scheme.Modules.pullback f).obj M, U) ≅ Γ(M, f ''ᵁ U) :=
2300	  (Scheme.Modules.toPresheaf X ⋙ (CategoryTheory.evaluation _ _).obj (Opposite.op U)).mapIso
2301	    ((Scheme.Modules.restrictFunctorIsoPullback f).symm.app M)
2302	
2303	/-- **The pullback-section comparison intertwines the restriction maps** (gap1, Hfr, naturality).
2304	For opens `V ≤ U` of `X`, `gammaPullbackImageIso` commutes with the presheaf restriction maps of
2305	`(pullback f).obj M` and of `M` (along the image inclusion `f ''ᵁ V ≤ f ''ᵁ U`). This is the
2306	naturality of the underlying morphism of abelian presheaves. -/
2307	theorem gammaPullbackImageIso_hom_naturality {X Y : Scheme.{u}} (f : X ⟶ Y) [IsOpenImmersion f]
2308	    (M : Y.Modules) {U V : X.Opens} (i : V ⟶ U) :
2309	    ((Scheme.Modules.pullback f).obj M).presheaf.map i.op ≫ (gammaPullbackImageIso f M V).hom
2310	      = (gammaPullbackImageIso f M U).hom ≫ M.presheaf.map (f.opensFunctor.map i).op := by
2311	  exact (((Scheme.Modules.restrictFunctorIsoPullback f).symm.app M).hom.mapPresheaf).naturality i.op
2312	
2313	/-- **Global sections of a pullback along an open immersion are sections over the range**
2314	(gap1, Hfr section transport). The `U = ⊤` instance of `gammaPullbackImageIso`:
2315	`Γ((pullback f).obj M, ⊤) ≅ Γ(M, f.opensRange)`, using `f ''ᵁ ⊤ = f.opensRange`. Once this lands the
2316	named-form descent `isLocalizedModule_basicOpen_descent` and gap1 follow. -/
2317	noncomputable def gammaPullbackTopIso {X Y : Scheme.{u}} (f : X ⟶ Y) [IsOpenImmersion f]
2318	    (M : Y.Modules) :
2319	    Γ((Scheme.Modules.pullback f).obj M, ⊤) ≅ Γ(M, f.opensRange) :=
2320	  gammaPullbackImageIso f M ⊤ ≪≫ eqToIso (by rw [Scheme.Hom.image_top_eq_opensRange])

2322	/-- **Open-immersion structure-sheaf ring iso on an image open** (gap1, Hfr semilinearity).
2323	For an open immersion `j : X ⟶ Y` and an open `V ⊆ X`, the immersion is an isomorphism onto its
2324	image `j ''ᵁ V`, so pulling structure-sheaf sections back gives a ring isomorphism
2325	`σ_V : Γ(X, V) ≃+* Γ(Y, j ''ᵁ V)`. This is `(j.appIso V)⁻¹` packaged as a `RingEquiv`; it is the
2326	`σ` along which `gammaPullbackImageIso_hom_semilinear` is semilinear, the form bridge (I)
2327	`isLocalizedModule_of_ringEquiv_semilinear` consumes.
2328	
2329	The direction is source `→` image (so `σ_V a` lands in `Γ(Y, j ''ᵁ V)` for `a : Γ(X, V)`, the
2330	section ring acting on the pullback module's sections). Project-local. -/
2331	noncomputable def gammaImageRingEquiv {X Y : Scheme.{u}} (j : X ⟶ Y) [IsOpenImmersion j]
2332	    (V : X.Opens) : Γ(X, V) ≃+* Γ(Y, j ''ᵁ V) :=
2333	  (j.appIso V).commRingCatIsoToRingEquiv.symm
2334	
2335	/-- **Semilinearity of the pullback section transport** (gap1 semilinearity wall). The forward map
2336	of `gammaPullbackImageIso` is `σ_V`-semilinear (`σ_V = gammaImageRingEquiv`): for `a : Γ(X, V)` a
2337	section of the structure sheaf and `x` a section of the pullback module,
2338	`hom (a • x) = σ_V a • hom x`. The pullback-side action is the structure-sheaf action through
2339	the pullback's `mapPresheaf`; the action on the `M` side is `M`'s action through `σ_V`.
2340	Project-local. -/
2341	theorem gammaPullbackImageIso_hom_semilinear {X Y : Scheme.{u}} (j : X ⟶ Y) [IsOpenImmersion j]
2342	    (M : Y.Modules) (V : X.Opens) (a : Γ(X, V))
2343	    (x : Γ((Scheme.Modules.pullback j).obj M, V)) :
2344	    (gammaPullbackImageIso j M V).hom (a • x)
2345	      = gammaImageRingEquiv j V a • (gammaPullbackImageIso j M V).hom x := by
2346	  -- `gammaPullbackImageIso j M V`'s forward map is `Γ(-, V)` of the `Ab`-morphism
2347	  -- `ψ := ((restrictFunctorIsoPullback j).symm.app M).hom`, i.e. the section map `ψ.app V`.
2348	  simp only [gammaPullbackImageIso, Functor.mapIso_hom, Functor.comp_map,
2349	    Scheme.Modules.toPresheaf_map, CategoryTheory.evaluation_obj_map,
2350	    Scheme.Modules.mapPresheaf_app]
2351	  -- `ψ.app V` is `Γ(X, V)`-linear (`Hom.app_smul`): `ψ.app V (a • x) = a • ψ.app V x`, the
2352	  -- `Γ(X, V)`-action being `restrictFunctor`'s `restrictScalars`-action along `(j.appIso V).inv`.
2353	  erw [Scheme.Modules.Hom.app_smul]
2354	  -- The `restrictScalars` action `a •_{restrict} m` is defeq to `(j.appIso V).inv a •_M m`,
2355	  -- and `σ_V a = gammaImageRingEquiv j V a = (j.appIso V).inv a`, so the two sides agree by `rfl`.
2356	  rfl

2358	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — gap1-D Hfr: combined algebra transport
2359	
2360	The two `IsLocalizedModule` bridges (I) `isLocalizedModule_of_ringEquiv_semilinear` and (II)
2361	`isLocalizedModule_restrictScalars_powers_algebraMap` are chained into a single transport lemma:
2362	the localization that P1 (`IsIso fromTildeΓ`) produces on the slice `Spec R_r` (a localization at
2363	`powers f'` over the section ring `S`) is read back, across the `σ`-semilinear section isos and the
2364	base change `R → A` (`A = R_r`), as a localization at `powers f` over the base ring `R`. -/
2365	
2366	/-- **(I)+(II) combined: ring-iso-semilinear localization transport descending to the base ring.**
2367	
2368	Given a base ring `R`, an `R`-algebra `A`, a ring iso `σ : S ≃+* A` carrying `f' : S` to
2369	`algebraMap R A f`, a localization `g` at `powers f'` over `S`, two `σ`-semilinear additive
2370	isomorphisms `e₁, e₂` onto `A`-modules (also `R`-modules via the scalar tower `R → A`), and an
2371	`A`-linear map `h` intertwining `g` with the `eᵢ` (`h (e₁ x) = e₂ (g x)`), the restriction of
2372	scalars of `h` to `R` is a localization at `powers f` over `R`.
2373	
2374	This is the algebra core of the gap1 `Hfr` transport: bridge (I)
2375	(`isLocalizedModule_of_ringEquiv_semilinear`) moves the localization across the ring iso to
2376	`powers (algebraMap R A f)` over `A`, then bridge (II)
2377	(`isLocalizedModule_restrictScalars_powers_algebraMap`) descends it to `powers f` over `R`.
2378	Project-local. -/
2379	theorem isLocalizedModule_powers_transport
2380	    {R A S : Type*} [CommRing R] [CommRing A] [CommRing S] [Algebra R A]
2381	    (σ : S ≃+* A) (f : R) (f' : S) (hf : σ f' = algebraMap R A f)
2382	    {M₁ M₂ N₁ N₂ : Type*}
2383	    [AddCommGroup M₁] [Module S M₁] [AddCommGroup M₂] [Module S M₂]
2384	    [AddCommGroup N₁] [Module A N₁] [Module R N₁] [IsScalarTower R A N₁]
2385	    [AddCommGroup N₂] [Module A N₂] [Module R N₂] [IsScalarTower R A N₂]
2386	    (g : M₁ →ₗ[S] M₂) [IsLocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers f') g]
2387	    (e₁ : M₁ ≃+ N₁) (e₂ : M₂ ≃+ N₂)
2388	    (he₁ : ∀ (a : S) (x : M₁), e₁ (a • x) = σ a • e₁ x)
2389	    (he₂ : ∀ (a : S) (x : M₂), e₂ (a • x) = σ a • e₂ x)
2390	    (h : N₁ →ₗ[A] N₂)

2708	(`isLocalizedModule_basicOpen`): for a quasi-coherent sheaf of modules `M` on an *arbitrary* scheme
2709	`X`, an affine open `U`, and `f : Γ(X, U)`, the section restriction `Γ(M, U) → Γ(M, D(f))` is
2710	`IsLocalizedModule (powers f)` over `Γ(X, U)`.

2713	`hU.fromSpec : Spec Γ(X, U) ⟶ X` (range `U`), so the pullback `M'` is quasi-coherent on
2714	`Spec Γ(X, U)`, where gap1 gives `IsIso M'.fromTildeΓ`; the engine
2715	`isLocalizedModule_restrict_of_isIso_fromTildeΓ` localizes the slice restriction over `Γ(X, U)`, and

2717	`isLocalizedModule_of_ringEquiv_semilinear`) transports it to the `M`-side restriction. No

2725	presheaf-of-modules restriction is semilinear, not bundled this way). -/

2729	  letI : Module Γ(X, U) Γ(M, V) := Module.compHom _ (X.presheaf.map i.op).hom

3183	a quasi-coherent sheaf on an arbitrary scheme.** For `M` quasi-coherent on `X`, an affine open `U`,
3184	and `f : Γ(X, U)`, the section restriction `Γ(M, U) → Γ(M, X.basicOpen f)` is
3185	`IsLocalizedModule (powers f)` over `Γ(X, U)`. Assembles the QC-pullback (Piece A,
3186	`isQuasicoherent_pullback_fromSpec`) → gap1 (`isIso_fromTildeΓ_of_isQuasicoherent`) → the eqToHom
3187	bridge (Piece B, `isLocalizedModule_basicOpen_of_hP1`). Project-local: closes gap2. -/
3188	theorem isLocalizedModule_basicOpen (M : X.Modules) [M.IsQuasicoherent]
3189	    {U : X.Opens} (hU : IsAffineOpen U) (f : Γ(X, U))
3190	    [Module Γ(X, U) Γ(M, X.basicOpen f)]
3191	    [IsScalarTower Γ(X, U) Γ(X, X.basicOpen f) Γ(M, X.basicOpen f)] :
3192	    IsLocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers f) (restrictBasicOpenₗ M f) :=
3193	  haveI := isQuasicoherent_pullback_fromSpec M hU
3194	  isLocalizedModule_basicOpen_of_hP1 M hU
3195	    (isIso_fromTildeΓ_of_isQuasicoherent ((Scheme.Modules.pullback hU.fromSpec).obj M)) f
3196	
3197	/-- Restriction maps of the underlying abelian presheaf of a sheaf of modules compose:
3198	restricting `A → B → C` equals the direct restriction `A → C`. -/
3199	private lemma res_res (M : X.Modules) {A B C : X.Opens} (hBA : B ≤ A) (hCB : C ≤ B)
3200	    (hCA : C ≤ A) (x : Γ(M, A)) :
3201	    M.presheaf.map (homOfLE hCB).op (M.presheaf.map (homOfLE hBA).op x)
3202	      = M.presheaf.map (homOfLE hCA).op x := by
3203	  rw [← ConcreteCategory.comp_apply, ← Functor.map_comp, ← op_comp]
3204	  rfl
3205	
3206	/-- Structure-sheaf version of `res_res`. -/
3207	private lemma resRing_res {A B C : X.Opens} (hBA : B ≤ A) (hCB : C ≤ B)
3208	    (hCA : C ≤ A) (g : Γ(X, A)) :
3209	    X.presheaf.map (homOfLE hCB).op (X.presheaf.map (homOfLE hBA).op g)
3210	      = X.presheaf.map (homOfLE hCA).op g := by
3211	  rw [← ConcreteCategory.comp_apply, ← Functor.map_comp, ← op_comp]
3212	  rfl
3213	
3214	/-- A section of a sheaf of modules over an open contained in `⊥` vanishes (separation over
3215	the empty cover). -/
3216	private lemma section_eq_zero_of_le_bot (M : X.Modules) {V : X.Opens} (hV : V ≤ ⊥)
3217	    (x : Γ(M, V)) : x = 0 :=
3218	  TopCat.Sheaf.eq_of_locally_eq' (⟨M.presheaf, M.isSheaf⟩ : TopCat.Sheaf Ab X)
3219	    (fun i : PEmpty.{u + 1} => i.elim) V (fun i => i.elim) (hV.trans bot_le) x 0
3220	    (fun i => i.elim)
3221	
3222	/-- **Torsion half of qcqs section localization** (Stacks 01P0-style, `exists_of_eq` engine).
3223	For a quasi-coherent sheaf of modules `M` on a scheme `X`, a section `g` of the structure sheaf
3224	over an open `W`, and a *quasi-compact* open `U ≤ W`, any section `x ∈ Γ(M, U)` that restricts
3225	to zero on `U ⊓ D(g)` is killed by a power of `g|_U`. Induction on the compact open `U`
3226	(`compact_open_induction_on`): the affine case is the gap2 keystone
3227	`isLocalizedModule_basicOpen`, and the step glues by sheaf separation over the two-element
3228	cover `{S, V}`. Project-local (Mathlib has no qcqs section-localization). -/
3229	theorem exists_pow_smul_res_eq_zero_of_isCompact
3230	    (M : X.Modules) [M.IsQuasicoherent] {W : X.Opens} (g : Γ(X, W))
3231	    (U : X.Opens) (hU : IsCompact (U : Set X)) :
3232	    ∀ (hUW : U ≤ W) (x : Γ(M, U)),
3233	      M.presheaf.map (homOfLE (inf_le_left : U ⊓ X.basicOpen g ≤ U)).op x = 0 →
3234	      ∃ n : ℕ, X.presheaf.map (homOfLE hUW).op g ^ n • x = 0 := by
3235	  refine compact_open_induction_on (P := fun U => ∀ (hUW : U ≤ W) (x : Γ(M, U)),
3236	      M.presheaf.map (homOfLE (inf_le_left : U ⊓ X.basicOpen g ≤ U)).op x = 0 →
3237	      ∃ n : ℕ, X.presheaf.map (homOfLE hUW).op g ^ n • x = 0) U hU ?_ ?_
3238	  · intro _ x _
3239	    exact ⟨0, by rw [pow_zero, one_smul]; exact section_eq_zero_of_le_bot M le_rfl x⟩

data.content:
apply CategoryTheory.Sheaf.hom_ext
    apply (TopCat.Sheaf.restrictHomEquivHom _ _ PrimeSpectrum.isBasis_basic_opens).symm.injective
    ext r : 3
    apply IsLocalizedModule.ext (.powers (M := R) r.unop)
      (toOpen _ (PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen (R := R) r.unop)).hom
    · rw [Subtype.forall]
      change Submonoid.powers _ ≤ (IsUnit.submonoid _).comap _
      simp only [Submonoid.powers_le, Submonoid.mem_comap, IsUnit.mem_submonoid_iff]
      exact Scheme.Modules.isUnit_algebraMap_end_of_le_basicOpen r.unop le_rfl
    dsimp [toTildeΓNatIso, isoTop,
      TopCat.Sheaf.restrictHomEquivHom, Functor.IsCoverDense.restrictHomEquivHom,
      fromTildeΓNatTrans, moduleSpecΓFunctor, Sheaf.forget, sheafToPresheaf]
    simp only [← ModuleCat.hom_comp, Functor.map_comp]
    congr 1
    rw [ObjectProperty.FullSubcategory.comp_hom]
    dsimp
    rw [toOpen_map_app_assoc, toOpen_fromTildeΓ_app]
    rfl
  right_triangle_components M := by
    dsimp [toTildeΓNatIso, fromTildeΓNatTrans, tilde.isoTop, moduleSpecΓFunctor, Sheaf.forget]
    rw [toOpen_fromTildeΓ_app]
    exact (modulesSpecToSheaf.obj M).obj.map_id _

instance : IsIso (tilde.adjunction (R := R)).unit := by
  dsimp [tilde.adjunction]; infer_instance

/-- The tilde functor is fully faithful. We will later show that the essential image is
exactly quasi-coherent modules. -/
def tilde.fullyFaithfulFunctor : (tilde.functor R).FullyFaithful :=
  tilde.adjunction.fullyFaithfulLOfIsIsoUnit

instance : (tilde.functor R).Full := tilde.fullyFaithfulFunctor.full
instance : (tilde.functor R).Faithful := tilde.fullyFaithfulFunctor.faithful
instance : (tilde.functor R).IsLeftAdjoint := tilde.adjunction.isLeftAdjoint
instance : (tilde.functor R).Additive :=
  have := Limits.preservesBinaryBiproducts_of_preservesBinaryCoproducts (tilde.functor R)
  Functor.additive_of_preservesBinaryBiproducts _

section

variable {M N : ModuleCat R} (f g : M ⟶ N)

@[simp] lemma tilde.map_zero : tilde.map (0 : M ⟶ N) = 0 :=
  (tilde.functor R).map_zero _ _

@[simp] lemma tilde.map_add : tilde.map (f + g) = tilde.map f + tilde.map g :=
  (tilde.functor R).map_add

@[simp] lemma tilde.map_sub : tilde.map (f - g) = tilde.map f - tilde.map g :=
  (tilde.functor R).map_sub

@[simp] lemma tilde.map_neg : tilde.map (-f) = - tilde.map f :=
  (tilde.functor R).map_neg

end

lemma isIso_fromTildeΓ_iff {M : (Spec R).Modules} :
    IsIso M.fromTildeΓ ↔ (tilde.functor R).essImage M :=
  tilde.adjunction.isIso_counit_app_iff_mem_essImage

section IsQuasicoherent

open Limits

/-- Tilde of `R` as an `R`-module is isomorphic to the structure sheaf `𝒪_{Spec R}`. -/
noncomputable
def tildeSelf : tilde (ModuleCat.of R R) ≅ SheafOfModules.unit.{u} _ := .refl _

instance : IsIso (Scheme.Modules.fromTildeΓ (SheafOfModules.unit.{u} (Spec R).ringCatSheaf)) :=
  isIso_fromTildeΓ_iff.mpr ⟨_, ⟨tildeSelf⟩⟩

/-- Tilde of direct sums of `R` as an `R`-module is isomorphic to the free sheaf. -/
noncomputable
def tildeFinsupp (ι : Type u) : tilde (ModuleCat.of R (ι →₀ R)) ≅ SheafOfModules.free.{u} ι :=
  letI H : IsColimit <| (tilde.functor R).mapCocone (ModuleCat.finsuppCocone R R ι) :=
    isColimitOfPreserves (tilde.functor R) (ModuleCat.finsuppCoconeIsColimit R R ι)
  letI iso : (Discrete.functor fun (_ : ι) ↦ ModuleCat.of R R) ⋙ tilde.functor R ≅
         Discrete.functor fun _ ↦ SheafOfModules.unit.{u} _ :=
      Discrete.natIso (fun _ ↦ tildeSelf)
  IsColimit.coconePointUniqueUpToIso
    ((IsColimit.precomposeHomEquiv iso.symm _).symm H) (coproductIsCoproduct _)

instance (ι : Type u) :
    IsIso (Scheme.Modules.fromTildeΓ (R := R) (SheafOfModules.free.{u} ι)) :=
  isIso_fromTildeΓ_iff.mpr ⟨_, ⟨tildeFinsupp _⟩⟩

set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
/-- Given a presentation of a module `M`, we may construct an associated presentation of `M^~`. -/
noncomputable
def presentationTilde (s : Set M) (hs : Submodule.span R s = ⊤)
    (t : Set (s →₀ R))
    (ht : Submodule.span R t = LinearMap.ker (Finsupp.linearCombination R ((↑) : s → M))) :
    (tilde M).Presentation := by
  haveI H₁ : Function.Exact
      (ModuleCat.ofHom (Finsupp.linearCombination (α := t) R (↑)))
      (ModuleCat.ofHom (Finsupp.linearCombination (α := s) (M := M) R (↑))) :=
    (LinearMap.exact_iff.mpr (by simp [Finsupp.range_linearCombination, ht]))
  refine SheafOfModules.presentationOfIsCokernelFree.{u}
      ((tildeFinsupp t).inv ≫ tilde.map (ModuleCat.ofHom (Finsupp.linearCombination R (↑))) ≫
        (tildeFinsupp s).hom) ((tildeFinsupp s).inv ≫
          tilde.map (ModuleCat.ofHom (Finsupp.linearCombination R (↑)))) (by
    simp only [Category.assoc, Iso.hom_inv_id_assoc, Preadditive.IsIso.comp_left_eq_zero]
    rw [← tilde.map_comp, ← ModuleCat.ofHom_comp]
    convert! tilde.map_zero
    exact congr(ModuleCat.ofHom $(H₁.linearMap_comp_eq_zero))) ?_
  letI h₁ := ModuleCat.isColimitCokernelCofork _ _ H₁
    (by simp [← LinearMap.range_eq_top, Finsupp.range_linearCombination, hs])
  refine IsCokernel.ofIso _ (CokernelCofork.mapIsColimit _ h₁ (tilde.functor R)) _ (tildeFinsupp t)
    (tildeFinsupp s) (.refl _) (by simp) (by simp)

instance : (tilde M).IsQuasicoherent :=
  (presentationTilde.{u} _ .univ (by simp) _ (Submodule.span_eq _)).isQuasicoherent

set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
lemma isIso_fromTildeΓ_of_presentation (M : (Spec R).Modules) (P : M.Presentation) :
    IsIso M.fromTildeΓ := by
  rw [isIso_fromTildeΓ_iff]
  let g := (tilde.functor _).preimage <| (tildeFinsupp _).hom ≫ P.relations.π ≫ kernel.ι _ ≫
    (tildeFinsupp _).inv
  let iso : cokernel ((tilde.functor R).map g) ≅ cokernel (P.relations.π ≫ kernel.ι _) := by
    refine cokernel.mapIso _ _ (tildeFinsupp _) (tildeFinsupp _) ?_
    simp only [g, (tilde.functor R).map_preimage]
    simp
  exact ⟨cokernel g, ⟨PreservesCokernel.iso (tilde.functor R) g ≪≫ iso ≪≫
    IsColimit.coconePointUniqueUpToIso (colimit.isColimit _) P.isColimit⟩⟩

section IsLocalizing

variable (M : (Spec R).Modules) (f : R) {S : CommRingCat.{u}} (φ : R ⟶ S)

open TopologicalSpace

4370	\subsection{Iter-189 analogist-licensed unbundle pins}
4371	\label{sec:quot_iter189_unbundle}
4372	
4373	The iter-189 \texttt{mathlib-analogist lane-f-isbasechange} verdict
4374	(see \texttt{analogies/lane-f-isbasechange.md}) returned verdict (A)
4375	\emph{STRUCTURAL OK} on the project's use of
4376	\texttt{IsBaseChange.of\_equiv}. The corrective was to \emph{unbundle}
4377	the iter-188 \(\Sigma\)-pair helper's three internal Mathlib gaps into
4378	separately named typed-sorry pins so that each can be targeted
4379	individually (~30-50 LOC apiece) in subsequent iters. Iter-189 Lane F
4380	prover landed the unbundle. The two new pins are recorded below
4381	(parallel to the existing \cref{lem:pullback_tildeIso} pin, which
4382	remains Step 2 of the 3-step chain).
4383	
4384	\begin{lemma}
4385	
4386	\leanok
4387	[Quasi-coherent module on an affine open is \texttt{tilde} of its sections (Stacks 01I8)]
4388	  \label{lem:tildeIso_of_isQuasicoherent_isAffineOpen}
4389	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.tildeIso_of_isQuasicoherent_isAffineOpen}
4390	  \uses{def:quot_canonical_basechange_map}
4391	  \textit{Source: Stacks~\href{https://stacks.math.columbia.edu/tag/01I8}{01I8}
4392	  (a quasi-coherent module on an affine scheme is \(\widetilde M\) for
4393	  some module).}
4394	  Let \(X\) be a scheme, \(\mathcal N\) a quasi-coherent
4395	  \(\mathcal O_X\)-module, and \(V \subset X\) an affine open. Then the
4396	  pullback of \(\mathcal N\) along \texttt{hV.fromSpec} is canonically
4397	  isomorphic to \texttt{tilde} of its sections:
4398	  \[
4399	    (\mathtt{Scheme.Modules.pullback}\,\mathtt{hV.fromSpec}).\mathrm{obj}\,
4400	      \mathcal N
4401	    \;\cong\;
4402	    \widetilde{\,\Gamma(\mathcal N, V)\,}
4403	    \quad\text{(on \(\mathrm{Spec}\,\Gamma(X, V)\)).}
4404	  \]
4405	  Mathlib \texttt{b80f227} ships the analog for \emph{free}
4406	  quasi-coherent modules (\texttt{isIso\_fromTildeΓ\_of\_presentation}
4407	  partial), but the general statement
4408	  ``\([N.IsQuasicoherent] \Rightarrow N|_V \cong \widetilde{\Gamma(N,V)}\)
4409	  on an affine open \(V\)'' (Stacks 01I8 \emph{lemma-affine-qc-module-iff})
4410	  is unowned at the \texttt{Scheme.Modules} layer.
4411	\end{lemma}
4412	\begin{proof}
4413	  \leanok
4414	  \uses{def:quot_canonical_basechange_map, lem:modules_pullback_mathlib,
4415	    lem:modules_restrictFunctorIsoPullback_mathlib}
4416	  Write \(j = \mathtt{hV.fromSpec} : \Spec\Gamma(X,V) \to X\) (an open
4417	  immersion with image \(V\)) and \(G = j^{*}\mathcal N\). Pullback along an
4418	  open immersion preserves quasi-coherence
4419	  (Lean \texttt{isQuasicoherent\_pullback\_fromSpec}), so \(G\) is a
4420	  quasi-coherent module on the affine scheme \(\Spec\Gamma(X,V)\); by the
4421	  affine structure theorem (Stacks 01I8, Lean
4422	  \texttt{isIso\_fromTildeΓ\_of\_isQuasicoherent}) the tilde--\(\Gamma\)
4423	  counit \(\widetilde{\Gamma(G,\top)} \to G\) is an isomorphism.
4424	
4425	  The canonical base map \(b : \Gamma(\mathcal N, V) \to \Gamma(G, \top)\)
4426	  (the \(V\)-sections of the pullback--pushforward adjunction unit followed by
4427	  restriction along \(\top \le j^{-1}V\)) is bijective. Indeed the two
4428	  adjunctions \(\mathrm{restrict} \dashv j_{*}\) and \(j^{*} \dashv j_{*}\)
4429	  share the right adjoint, so their units are identified through the canonical
4430	  natural isomorphism \(\mathrm{restrict} \cong j^{*}\)
4431	  (\cref{lem:modules_restrictFunctorIsoPullback_mathlib}). Under this
4432	  identification, the \(V\)-component of the unit of the restriction
4433	  adjunction is the presheaf restriction of \(\mathcal N\) along the
4434	  \emph{equality} of opens \(j(j^{-1}V) = V\), hence bijective; the component
4435	  of the natural isomorphism is bijective; and the final restriction is along
4436	  the equality \(\top = j^{-1}V\). Thus \(b\) is a composite of three
4437	  bijections.
4438	
4439	  Since \(b\) is a bijective \(\Gamma(X,V)\)-linear map (the
4440	  \(\Gamma(X,V)\)-action on \(\Gamma(G,\top)\) being through the canonical
4441	  ring isomorphism \(\Gamma(X,V) \cong \Gamma(\Spec\Gamma(X,V),\top)\)),
4442	  it induces an isomorphism
4443	  \(\widetilde{b} : \widetilde{\Gamma(\mathcal N,V)} \cong
4444	  \widetilde{\Gamma(G,\top)}\). The required isomorphism is the inverse of
4445	  \(\widetilde{b}\) followed by the counit. The characterizing section
4446	  identity holds because \(\widetilde{(-)}\)-functoriality intertwines the
4447	  canonical maps \(M \to \Gamma(\widetilde M, U)\) (naturality of
4448	  \texttt{toOpen}), while the counit composed with \(\mathtt{toOpen}\) at
4449	  \(\top\) is the identity restriction.
4450	\end{proof}
4451	
4452	\begin{lemma}
4453	
4454	\leanok
4455	[Section-level transport for pullback along an affine open's
4456	\texttt{fromSpec}]
4457	  \label{lem:pullback_of_openImmersion_iso_restrict}
4458	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.pullback_of_openImmersion_iso_restrict}
4459	  \uses{def:quot_canonical_basechange_map}
4460	  \textit{Source: Project-bespoke transport, formed from
4461	  Stacks~\href{https://stacks.math.columbia.edu/tag/01HH}{01HH} +
4462	  \texttt{tilde.isoTop}.}
4463	  Let \(Y\) be a scheme, \(\mathcal N\) an \(\mathcal O_Y\)-module, and
4464	  \(U \subset Y\) an affine open. Equip \(\Gamma((\mathrm{Spec}\,\Gamma(Y, U)),
4465	  \top)\) with the \(\Gamma(Y, U)\)-algebra structure inherited from
4466	  \texttt{Scheme.ΓSpecIso} (the canonical
4467	  \(\Gamma(\mathrm{Spec}\,R, \top) \cong R\) for any commutative ring \(R\)),
4468	  and equip
4469	  \(\Gamma((\text{pullback}\,\mathtt{hU.fromSpec}).\mathrm{obj}\,\mathcal N,
4470	  \top)\) with the \(\Gamma(Y, U)\)-module structure inherited via
4471	  \texttt{Module.compHom}. Then there is a canonical \(\Gamma(Y, U)\)-linear
4472	  isomorphism between this pullback-section module and the original section
4473	  module \(\Gamma(\mathcal N, U)\):
4474	  \[
4475	    \Gamma((\text{pullback}\,\mathtt{hU.fromSpec}).\mathrm{obj}\,\mathcal N,
4476	      \top)
4477	    \;\simeq_{\Gamma(Y, U)}\;
4478	    \Gamma(\mathcal N, U).
4479	  \]
4480	  The map identifies a section of the pulled-back sheaf at \(\top\) of
4481	  \(\mathrm{Spec}\,\Gamma(Y, U)\) with the corresponding section of
4482	  \(\mathcal N\) on the affine open \(U \subset Y\). This pin is the
4483	  iter-190+ prover target as Step 3 of the iter-189 \(\Sigma\)-pair
4484	  unbundle.
4485	\end{lemma}
4486	\begin{proof}
4487	  \leanok
4488	  \uses{def:quot_canonical_basechange_map,
4489	    lem:modules_restrictFunctorIsoPullback_mathlib}
4490	  The underlying additive equivalence is the composite of the
4491	  \(\top\)-section of the natural isomorphism
4492	  \(j^{*} \cong \mathrm{restrict}\,j\) for the open immersion
4493	  \(j = \mathtt{hU.fromSpec}\)
4494	  (\cref{lem:modules_restrictFunctorIsoPullback_mathlib}) with the
4495	  restriction of \(\mathcal N\) along the equality of opens
4496	  \(j(\top) = U\); its \(\Gamma(Y, U)\)-linearity reduces (through
4497	  \texttt{Hom.app\_smul} and semilinearity of section restriction) to
4498	  the ring identity
4499	  \(\iota^{-1} \circ (j^{\sharp}\top)^{-1} \circ
4500	    \mathrm{res}_{U = j(\top)} = \mathrm{id}\), which follows from
4501	  \texttt{fromSpec\_app\_self}.
4502	
4503	  The additional \(\Sigma\)-pair characterization (T12, 2026-07-03) —
4504	  the inverse of the equivalence is the canonical base map — is the
4505	  unit-compatibility of \texttt{Adjunction.leftAdjointUniq} for the
4506	  restriction adjunction versus the pullback--pushforward adjunction,
4507	  as spelled out in \cref{lem:baseMap_inv_step3_open_immersion}.
4508	\end{proof}
4509

4511	\label{sec:quot_iter195_bc_substrate}
4512	
4513	The iter-195 plan-phase refactor \texttt{lane-f-step12-sigma-pair}
4514	upgraded the helpers \texttt{step1} and \texttt{step2} of the

---

\begin{lemma}
\label{lemma-widetilde-pullback}
Let
$(X, \mathcal{O}_X) = (\Spec(S), \mathcal{O}_{\Spec(S)})$,
$(Y, \mathcal{O}_Y) = (\Spec(R), \mathcal{O}_{\Spec(R)})$
be affine schemes.
Let $\psi : (X, \mathcal{O}_X) \to (Y, \mathcal{O}_Y)$ be a
morphism of affine schemes, corresponding to the ring map
$\psi^\sharp : R \to S$ (see Lemma \ref{lemma-category-affine-schemes}).
\begin{enumerate}
\item We have $\psi^* \widetilde M = \widetilde{S \otimes_R M}$
functorially in the $R$-module $M$.
\item We have $\psi_* \widetilde N = \widetilde{N_R}$ functorially
in the $S$-module $N$.
\end{enumerate}
\end{lemma}

\begin{proof}
The first assertion follows from the identification in
Lemma \ref{lemma-compare-constructions}
and the result of Modules, Lemma \ref{modules-lemma-restrict-quasi-coherent}.
The second assertion follows from the fact
that $\psi^{-1}(D(f)) = D(\psi^\sharp(f))$ and hence
$$
\psi_* \widetilde N(D(f)) = \widetilde N(D(\psi^\sharp(f))) =
N_{\psi^\sharp(f)} = (N_R)_f = \widetilde{N_R}(D(f))
$$
as desired.
\end{proof}

\noindent
Lemma \ref{lemma-widetilde-pullback} above says in particular
that if you restrict
the sheaf $\widetilde M$ to a standard affine open subspace
$D(f)$, then you get $\widetilde{M_f}$. We will use this from
now on without further mention.

\begin{lemma}
\label{lemma-quasi-coherent-affine}
Let $(X, \mathcal{O}_X) = (\Spec(R), \mathcal{O}_{\Spec(R)})$
be an affine scheme. Let $\mathcal{F}$ be a
quasi-coherent $\mathcal{O}_X$-module. Then
$\mathcal{F}$ is isomorphic to the sheaf associated to
the $R$-module $\Gamma(X, \mathcal{F})$.
\end{lemma}

\begin{proof}
Let $\mathcal{F}$ be a quasi-coherent $\mathcal{O}_X$-module.
Since every standard open $D(f)$ is quasi-compact we see that
$X$ is locally quasi-compact, i.e., every point has a fundamental
system of quasi-compact neighbourhoods, see Topology,
Definition \ref{topology-definition-locally-quasi-compact}.
Hence by Modules, Lemma \ref{modules-lemma-quasi-coherent-module}
for every prime $\mathfrak p \subset R$ corresponding to $x \in X$
there exists an open neighbourhood $x \in U \subset X$ such that
$\mathcal{F}|_U$ is isomorphic to the quasi-coherent
sheaf associated to some $\mathcal{O}_X(U)$-module $M$.
In other words, we get an open covering by $U$'s with this property.
By Lemma \ref{lemma-standard-open} for example we can refine this
covering to  a standard open covering.
Thus we get a covering $\Spec(R) = \bigcup D(f_i)$
and $R_{f_i}$-modules $M_i$ and isomorphisms
$\varphi_i : \mathcal{F}|_{D(f_i)} \to \mathcal{F}_{M_i}$
for some $R_{f_i}$-module $M_i$. On the overlaps
we get isomorphisms
$$
\xymatrix{
\mathcal{F}_{M_i}|_{D(f_if_j)}
\ar[rr]^{\varphi_i^{-1}|_{D(f_if_j)}}
& &
\mathcal{F}|_{D(f_if_j)}
\ar[rr]^{\varphi_j|_{D(f_if_j)}}
& &
\mathcal{F}_{M_j}|_{D(f_if_j)}.
}
$$
Let us denote these $\psi_{ij}$. It is clear that
we have the cocycle condition
$$
\psi_{jk}|_{D(f_if_jf_k)}
\circ
\psi_{ij}|_{D(f_if_jf_k)}
=
\psi_{ik}|_{D(f_if_jf_k)}
$$
on triple overlaps.

\medskip\noindent
Recall that each of the open subspaces $D(f_i)$, $D(f_if_j)$,
$D(f_if_jf_k)$ is an affine scheme. Hence the sheaves $\mathcal{F}_{M_i}$
are isomorphic to the sheaves $\widetilde M_i$ by Lemma
\ref{lemma-compare-constructions} above. In particular we see that
$\mathcal{F}_{M_i}(D(f_if_j)) = (M_i)_{f_j}$, etc.
Also by Lemma \ref{lemma-compare-constructions} above we see
that $\psi_{ij}$ corresponds to a unique $R_{f_if_j}$-module isomorphism
$$
\psi_{ij} : (M_i)_{f_j} \longrightarrow (M_j)_{f_i}
$$
namely, the effect of $\psi_{ij}$ on sections over $D(f_if_j)$.
